calculators.name: Body Mass Index
calculators.description: BMI is a measure of body fat based on height and weight.
calculators.transparency_template:
BMI = weight (kg) / (height (m))²

= {weightKg} / ({heightCm} / 100)²
= {weightKg} / ({heightM})²
= {weightKg} / {heightMSquared}
= {bmi} kg/m²
calculators.source: WHO. Physical status: the use and interpretation of anthropometry. WHO Technical Report Series 854. 1995.

calculators.name: Body Surface Area
calculators.description: BSA is used for drug dosing and cardiac output indexing.
calculators.transparency_template:
Mosteller: BSA = √(height (cm) × weight (kg) / 3600)
= {mosteller} m²

DuBois: BSA = 0.007184 × weight⁰·⁴²⁵ × height⁰·⁷²⁵
= {dubois} m²
calculators.source: Mosteller RD. Simplified calculation of body-surface area. N Engl J Med. 1987;317(17):1098.

calculators.name: Ideal Body Weight
calculators.description: IBW is used for drug dosing calculations. Uses the Devine formula.
calculators.source: Devine BJ. Gentamicin therapy. Drug Intell Clin Pharm. 1974;8:650-655.

calculators.name: Mean Arterial Pressure
calculators.description: MAP is the average arterial pressure during a single cardiac cycle.
calculators.transparency_template:
MAP = Diastolic + (Systolic − Diastolic) / 3
= {diastolic} + ({systolic} − {diastolic}) / 3
= {map} mmHg
calculators.source: Sesso HD et al. Systolic and diastolic blood pressures as predictors of cardiovascular outcomes. Hypertension. 2000;36(5):801-807.

calculators.name: QTc Interval (Corrected QT)
calculators.description: QTc is the QT interval corrected for heart rate.
calculators.transparency_template:
RR Interval = 60 / HR = 60 / {heartRate} = {rrInterval} s

Bazett: QTc = QT / √RR = {bazett} ms
Fridericia: QTc = QT / ³√RR = {fridericia} ms

interpretations.label: Critically prolonged — high risk of Torsades de Pointes

calculators.source: Bazett HC. An analysis of the time-relations of electrocardiograms. Heart. 1920;7:353-370.

calculators.name: Cardiac Output
calculators.description: CO is the volume of blood the heart pumps per minute.
calculators.transparency_template:
CO = HR × SV = {heartRate} × {strokeVolume} = {co} L/min
{ciLine}
calculators.source: Porter A et al. CO: physiologic clinical considerations. Anaesthesia. 2012;67(7):762-771.

calculators.name: eGFR (Cockcroft-Gault)
calculators.description: Estimates creatinine clearance.
calculators.transparency_template:
eGFR = ((140 − Age) × Weight) / (72 × Cr) × Sex Factor
= {egfr} mL/min
calculators.source: Cockcroft DW, Gault MH. Prediction of creatinine clearance from serum creatinine. Nephron. 1976;16(1):31-41.

calculators.name: Anion Gap
calculators.description: Helps distinguish causes of metabolic acidosis.
calculators.transparency_template: AG = Na⁺ − (Cl⁻ + HCO₃⁻) = {sodium} − ({chloride} + {bicarbonate}) = {ag} mEq/L

interpretations.label: Elevated — consider MUDPILES causes
interpretations.detail: M — Methanol, U — Uremia, D — DKA, P — Paraldehyde, I — Isoniazid/Infection, L — Lactic acidosis, E — Ethylene glycol, S — Salicylates

calculators.source: Emmett M. Anion-gap interpretation: the old and the new. Clin Chem. 1977;23(2 Pt 1):244-247.

calculators.name: FeNa (Fractional Excretion of Sodium)
calculators.description: Differentiates prerenal azotemia from intrinsic renal injury.
calculators.transparency_template: FeNa = (UNa × SCr) / (SNa × UCr) × 100% = {fena}%
calculators.source: Espinel CH. The FeNa test. Use in the differential diagnosis of acute renal failure. JAMA. 1976;236(6):579-581.

calculators.name: IV Drip Rate
calculators.description: Calculates IV drip rate in drops per minute. Select the giving set type or enter a custom drop factor.
calculators.transparency_template:
Drip rate = (Volume × Drop Factor) / (Time × 60)
= ({volumeMl} × {df}) / ({timeHours} × 60)
= {drip_rate_exact} ≈ {drip_rate} gtt/min
calculators.source: Perry AG, Potter PA. Clinical Nursing Skills and Techniques. 9th ed. Elsevier. 2017.

calculators.name: Maintenance Fluids (4-2-1 Rule)
calculators.description: Calculates maintenance fluid requirements using the Holliday-Segar (4-2-1) rule.
calculators.transparency_template:
4-2-1 Rule:
≤10kg: {tier1} mL/hr
11-20kg: {tier2} mL/hr
>20kg: {tier3} mL/hr

Hourly: {hourly_rate} mL/hr
Daily: {hourly_rate} × 24 = {daily_rate} mL/day
calculators.source: Holliday MA, Segar WE. The maintenance need for water in parenteral fluid therapy. Pediatrics. 1957;19(5):823-832.

calculators.name: Parkland Formula (Burns)
calculators.description: The Parkland formula estimates fluid resuscitation requirements in the first 24 hours for burn patients.
calculators.transparency_template:
Total 24h = 4 × Weight × TBSA% = 4 × {weightKg} × {tbsaPercent} = {total_24hr} mL

First 8h: Half = {half_total} mL → Rate = {first_8hr_rate} mL/hr
Next 16h: Rate = {next_16hr_rate} mL/hr
calculators.source: Baxter CR, Shires T. Physiological response to crystalloid resuscitation of severe burns. Ann N Y Acad Sci. 1968;150(3):874-894.

calculators.name: Weight Estimation by Age
calculators.description: Estimates weight for children aged 1–12 years.
calculators.transparency_template:
Nelson's (≤5): Weight = (Age × 2) + 8 = {nelson} kg
APLS (>5): Weight = (Age + 4) × 2 = {apls} kg
Recommended: {recommended} kg
calculators.source: Nelson WE. Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics. 21st ed. Elsevier. 2020.

calculators.name: Paediatric eGFR (Schwartz)
calculators.description: Estimates GFR in children using the Bedside Schwartz equation.
calculators.transparency_template: eGFR = 0.413 × Height / Cr = 0.413 × {heightCm} / {serumCreatinine} = {schwartz} mL/min/1.73m²
calculators.source: Schwartz GJ et al. A simple estimate of glomerular filtration rate in full-term infants during the first year of life. J Pediatr. 1984;104(6):849-854. (Updated bedside Schwartz 2009)

calculators.name: Paediatric Drug Dosing
calculators.description: Calculates paediatric drug doses based on weight, dose per kg, frequency, and optional maximum single dose cap.
calculators.source: Takemoto CK et al. Pediatric Dosage Handbook. Lexicomp. Annual ed.

calculators.name: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS)
calculators.description: GCS assesses level of consciousness. Score 3–15.
calculators.transparency_template: GCS = E + V + M = {eye} + {verbal} + {motor} = {total}/15
calculators.source: Teasdale G, Jennett B. Assessment of coma and impaired consciousness. A practical scale. Lancet. 1974;2(7872):81-84.

calculators.name: NIH Stroke Scale (NIHSS)
calculators.description: 11-item neurological assessment for stroke severity. Score 0–42.

results.label: Total NIHSS
results.formula: loc + locQuestions + locCommands + gaze + visual + facial + motorArm + motorLeg + ataxia + sensory + language + dysarthria + inattention

calculators.transparency_template: NIHSS = sum of 11 items = {total}/42
calculators.source: Brott T et al. Measurements of acute cerebral infarction: a clinical examination scale. Stroke. 1989;20(7):864-870.

calculators.name: Revised Trauma Score (RTS)
calculators.description: Physiologic triage tool using GCS, SBP, and respiratory rate.
calculators.source: Champion HR et al. A revision of the Trauma Score. J Trauma. 1989;29(5):623-629.

calculators.name: CURB-65 (Pneumonia Severity)
calculators.description: Pneumonia severity and hospital admission guide. Score 0–5.
calculators.transparency_template:
CURB-65 = Confusion + Urea + RR + BP + Age
= {confusion} + {urea} + {respRate} + {systolicBP} + {age65} = {total}/5

interpretations.label: High severity — urgent hospital admission, consider ICU (mortality >15%)

calculators.source: Lim WS et al. Defining community acquired pneumonia severity on presentation to hospital: an international derivation and validation study. Thorax. 2003;58(5):377-382.

calculators.name: Wells Score for PE
calculators.description: Pre-test probability of pulmonary embolism.

inputs.label: PE is #1 diagnosis or equally likely

results.label: Total Wells
results.formula: dvtSigns + peMostLikely + (heartRate > 100 ? 1.5 : 0) + immobilisation + priorDvtPe + haemoptysis + malignancy

interpretations.label: PE likely — consider CT pulmonary angiography

calculators.source: Wells PS et al. Derivation of a simple clinical model to categorize patients with suspected PE. Thromb Haemost. 2000;83(3):416-420.

calculators.name: PERC Rule (PE Rule-Out)
calculators.description: If ALL 8 criteria are absent AND pre-test probability is low, PE can be excluded without D-dimer.

inputs.label: Recent surgery or trauma (≤4 weeks, requiring GA)

results.label: Positive criteria
results.formula: age50 + hr100 + spo2 + legSwelling + haemoptysis + recentSurgery + priorPeDvt + oestrogen

results.label: PERC negative (all absent)
results.formula: (age50 + hr100 + spo2 + legSwelling + haemoptysis + recentSurgery + priorPeDvt + oestrogen) == 0 ? 1 : 0

calculators.transparency_template:
PERC criteria present: {positiveCount}/8
{percNegative == 1 ? 'PERC negative → PE excluded in low pre-test probability' : 'PERC positive → proceed to D-dimer or imaging'}

interpretations.label: PERC negative — PE excluded in low pre-test probability
PERC positive — proceed to D-dimer or imaging

calculators.source: Kline JA et al. Clinical criteria to prevent unnecessary diagnostic testing in emergency department patients with suspected pulmonary embolism. J Thromb Haemost. 2004;2(8):1247-1255.

calculators.name: A-a Gradient
calculators.description: Alveolar-arterial gradient differentiates causes of hypoxaemia.
calculators.transparency_template:
PAO₂ = (FiO₂ × (P_atm − P_H₂O)) − (PaCO₂ / RQ)
= ({fio2}% × ({patm} − 47)) − ({paCO2} / 0.8)
= {pao2Alveolar} mmHg

A-a Gradient = PAO₂ − PaO₂ = {pao2Alveolar} − {paO2}
= {aaGradient} mmHg

Normal upper limit (Age/4 + 4) = {normalUpper} mmHg

interpretations.label: Elevated — consider V/Q mismatch, shunt, or diffusion defect
Normal — consider hypoventilation or low FiO₂

calculators.source: Harris EA et al. The normal alveolar-arterial oxygen-tension gradient in man. Clin Sci Mol Med. 1974;46(1):89-93.

calculators.name: PaO₂/FiO₂ Ratio (ARDS Severity)
calculators.description: Berlin Definition of ARDS severity.
calculators.transparency_template: P/F Ratio = PaO₂ / FiO₂ = {paO2} / ({fio2} / 100) = {ratio}
calculators.source: ARDS Definition Task Force. Acute respiratory distress syndrome: the Berlin Definition. JAMA. 2012;307(23):2526-2533.

calculators.name: qSOFA Score
calculators.description: Quick bedside sepsis screening. No labs needed. Score 0–3.
calculators.transparency_template:
qSOFA = (RR≥22) + (GCS<15) + (SBP≤100)
= {rrPoint} + {gcsPoint} + {bpPoint} = {total}/3

interpretations.label: High risk of poor outcome from infection — escalate care

calculators.source: Singer M et al. The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA. 2016;315(8):801-810.

calculators.name: SOFA Score (Sequential Organ Failure Assessment)
calculators.description: Six organ systems scored 0–4 each. Total 0–24.

results.label: Total SOFA
results.formula: respiratory + coagulation + liver + cardiovascular + cns + renal

calculators.source: Vincent JL et al. The SOFA (Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment) score to describe organ dysfunction/failure. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710.

calculators.name: SIRS Criteria
calculators.description: Systemic Inflammatory Response Syndrome. ≥2 criteria = SIRS positive.
calculators.source: Bone RC et al. Definitions for sepsis and organ failure and guidelines for the use of innovative therapies in sepsis. Chest. 1992;101(6):1644-1655.

calculators.name: Shock Index
calculators.description: Simple haemodynamic marker. Normal: 0.5–0.7.
calculators.transparency_template: Shock Index = HR / SBP = {heartRate} / {systolicBP} = {si}
calculators.source: Allgower M, Burri C. Schockindex. Dtsch Med Wochenschr. 1967;92(43):1947-1950.

calculators.name: APACHE II Score
calculators.description: Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation II. Predicts ICU mortality.

options.label: 5 — Emergency post-op or non-operative with organ insufficiency

calculators.source: Knaus WA et al. APACHE II: a severity of disease classification system. Crit Care Med. 1985;13(10):818-829.

calculators.name: Estimated Due Date (Naegele's Rule)
calculators.description: Calculate EDD and gestational age from days since last menstrual period (LMP). LMP + 280 days.
calculators.source: Naegele FC. Erfahrungen und Abhandlungen aus dem Gebiete der Krankheiten des weiblichen Geschlechtes. Mannheim: Loeffler. 1812.

calculators.name: APGAR Score
calculators.description: Neonatal assessment at 1 and 5 minutes after birth. Score 0–10.
calculators.transparency_template:
APGAR = Appearance + Pulse + Grimace + Activity + Respiration
= {appearance} + {pulse} + {grimace} + {activity} + {respiration} = {total}/10

interpretations.label: Moderate depression — stimulation and supplemental O₂

calculators.source: Apgar V. A proposal for a new method of evaluation of the newborn infant. Anesth Analg. 1953;32(4):260-267.

calculators.name: PPH Blood Loss Estimator
calculators.description: Estimates blood loss and guides transfusion management in postpartum haemorrhage.
calculators.transparency_template:
EBV (pregnancy) = Weight × 100 = {weightKg} × 100 = {ebv} mL
Estimated loss = ({percentLoss}% / 100) × {ebv} = {estimatedLossMl} mL

interpretations.label: Class I — Compensated, no transfusion needed

calculators.source: Bose P et al. Blood loss estimation in obstetrics: a comparative study. BJOG. 2003;110(2):155-160.

calculators.name: Child-Pugh Score
calculators.description: Liver cirrhosis severity. Guides prognosis and surgical risk. Score 5–15.
calculators.source: Child CG, Turcotte JG. Surgery and portal hypertension. Major Probl Clin Surg. 1964;1:1-85. (Modified by Pugh 1973)

calculators.name: MELD Score
calculators.description: Model for End-Stage Liver Disease. Predicts 3-month mortality. Score 6–40.
calculators.source: Malinchoc M et al. A model to predict poor survival in patients undergoing transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunts. Hepatology. 2000;31(4):864-871.

calculators.name: Ranson's Criteria (Pancreatitis)
calculators.description: Pancreatitis severity. Admission + 48-hour criteria. Score 0–11.

results.label: 48-hour criteria
results.formula: hematocritFall + bunRise + calcium + paO2 + baseDeficit + fluidSeq

results.label: Total Ranson
results.formula: age55 + wbc + glucose + ldh + ast + hematocritFall + bunRise + calcium + paO2 + baseDeficit + fluidSeq

calculators.source: Ranson JH et al. Prognostic signs and the role of operative management in acute pancreatitis. Surg Gynecol Obstet. 1974;139(1):69-81.

calculators.name: Glasgow-Blatchford Score (Upper GI Bleed)
calculators.description: Predicts need for endoscopic intervention before endoscopy. Score 0–23.

results.label: Total Glasgow-Blatchford
results.formula: urea + (isMale == true ? (hb >= 13 ? 0 : (hb >= 12 ? 1 : (hb >= 10 ? 3 : 6))) : (hb >= 12 ? 0 : (hb >= 10 ? 1 : 6))) + (systolicBP >= 110 ? 0 : (systolicBP >= 100 ? 1 : (systolicBP >= 90 ? 2 : 3))) + pulse100 + melaena + syncope + hepatic + cardiacFailure

interpretations.label: Very low risk — outpatient management may be appropriate

calculators.source: Blatchford O et al. A risk score to predict need for treatment for upper-gastrointestinal haemorrhage. Lancet. 2000;356(9238):1318-1321.

calculators.name: Rockall Score (GI Bleed Re-bleeding Risk)
calculators.description: Predicts re-bleeding and mortality after endoscopy for upper GI bleed.

options.label: 3 — Renal failure, liver failure, metastatic cancer

calculators.source: Rockall TA et al. Risk assessment after acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage. Gut. 1996;38(3):316-321.

calculators.name: Corrected Calcium
calculators.description: Hypoalbuminaemia artificially lowers measured calcium. Formula: Corrected Ca = Measured Ca + 0.8 × (4 − Albumin).
calculators.transparency_template:
Corrected Ca = Measured Ca + 0.8 × (4 − Albumin)
= {measuredCa} + 0.8 × (4 − {albumin})
= {corrected} mg/dL = {correctedMmol} mmol/L
calculators.source: Payne RB et al. Interpretation of serum calcium in patients with abnormal serum proteins. Br Med J. 1973;4(5893):643-646.

calculators.name: Transfusion Volume Calculator
calculators.description: Calculates volume of pRBCs needed to reach target haemoglobin.
calculators.transparency_template:
Volume (mL) = (Target Hb − Current Hb) × Weight × 3
= ({targetHb} − {currentHb}) × {weightKg} × 3
= {volumeMl} mL ≈ {estimatedUnits} units
(1 unit pRBC ≈ 300 mL, raises Hb by ~1 g/dL in 70 kg adult)
calculators.source: WHO. The clinical use of blood in medicine, obstetrics, paediatrics, surgery and anaesthesia, trauma and burns. 2001.

calculators.name: HbA1c ↔ eAG Converter
calculators.description: Bidirectional converter between HbA1c (%) and estimated Average Glucose (mg/dL and mmol/L).
calculators.transparency_template:
eAG (mg/dL) = 28.7 × HbA1c − 46.7
= 28.7 × {hba1c} − 46.7
= {eagMgDl} mg/dL = {eagMmolL} mmol/L
calculators.source: Nathan DM et al. Translating the A1C assay into estimated average glucose values. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(8):1473-1478.

calculators.name: Serum Osmolality
calculators.description: Calculates serum osmolality and osmolar gap. Normal: 275–295 mOsm/kg.
calculators.transparency_template:
Osmolality = 2×Na + Glucose/18 + BUN/2.8
= 2×{sodium} + {glucose}/18 + {bun}/2.8
= {calculated} mOsm/kg
{measuredOsm > 0 ? 'Osmolar gap = ' + measuredOsm + ' − ' + calculated + ' = ' + osmolarGap + ' mOsm/kg (normal <10)' : 'Provide measured osmolality for osmolar gap.'}

interpretations.label: Elevated — consider hypernatraemia, hyperglycaemia, or toxin

calculators.source: Dorwart WV, Chalmers L. Comparison of methods for calculating serum osmolality from chemical concentrations, and the prognostic value of such calculations. Clin Chem. 1975;21(2):190-194.

calculators.name: Corrected Sodium (Hyperglycaemia)
calculators.description: Corrects measured Na for dilution by high glucose in DKA and HHS.
calculators.transparency_template:
Corrected Na = Measured Na + 1.6 × (Glucose − 100) / 100
= {measuredNa} + 1.6 × ({glucose} − 100) / 100
= {measuredNa} + {correction}
= {correctedNa} mEq/L
calculators.source: Katz MA. Hyperglycemia-induced hyponatremia — calculation of expected serum sodium depression. N Engl J Med. 1973;289(16):843-844.

calculators.name: Bicarbonate Deficit
calculators.description: Calculates HCO₃⁻ replacement in metabolic acidosis. Give 50% of deficit over 3–4 hours.
calculators.transparency_template:
HCO₃⁻ Deficit = 0.4 × Weight × (Target − Actual)
= 0.4 × {weightKg} × ({targetHCO3 > 0 ? targetHCO3 : 15} − {actualHCO3})
= {deficit} mEq total

Give {initialDose} mEq (50%) over 3–4 hours. Recheck ABG.
WARNING: Do NOT fully correct rapidly — risk of metabolic alkalosis.
calculators.source: Narins RG, Emmett M. Simple and mixed acid-base disorders: a practical approach. Medicine (Baltimore). 1980;59(3):161-187.

calculators.name: Burch-Wartofsky Score (Thyroid Storm)
calculators.description: Differentiates thyroid storm from severe thyrotoxicosis. Score ≥45 = thyroid storm.

results.label: Total Burch-Wartofsky
results.formula: temperature + cns + gi + hrPoint + afib + chf + precipitant

interpretations.label: Thyroid storm — immediate intensive treatment required

calculators.source: Burch HB, Wartofsky L. Life-threatening thyrotoxicosis. Thyroid storm. Endocrinol Metab Clin North Am. 1993;22(2):263-277.

calculators.name: Malaria Parasite Density
calculators.description: WHO-recommended: count parasites against WBCs on thick film. Default WBC = 8,000/uL if unknown.

interpretations.label: Moderate parasitaemia - monitor for severe features
High parasitaemia - consider severe malaria criteria

calculators.source: WHO. Basic Malaria Microscopy. 2nd ed. 2010. Part I. Learner’s Guide.

calculators.name: Cholera Dehydration (WHO Plans A/B/C)
calculators.description: WHO classification of dehydration severity in cholera. Determines treatment plan.

options.label: Unable to drink or drinks poorly

interpretations.label: Plan A - No dehydration: ORS + zinc at home
interpretations.detail: Give ORS after each loose stool. <24mo: 50-100mL, 2-10yr: 100-200mL. Zinc 20mg x10 days.

interpretations.label: Plan B - Some dehydration: 75 mL/kg ORS over 4h
interpretations.detail: Give ORS 75 mL/kg over 4 hours. Monitor frequently.

interpretations.label: Plan C - Severe dehydration: IV fluids STAT
interpretations.detail: Infants: 30 mL/kg IV in 1h then 70 mL/kg over 5h. Older: 30 mL/kg in 30min then 70 mL/kg over 2.5h. RL or NS.

calculators.source: WHO. The Treatment of Diarrhoea — A Manual for Physicians and Other Senior Health Workers. 2005.

calculators.name: Cholera Fluid Replacement (Plan C)
calculators.description: IV fluid requirements for severe cholera dehydration per WHO Plan C.
calculators.transparency_template:
WHO Plan C - Severe Dehydration:

Initial: 30 mL/kg
= 30 x {weightKg} = {initialBolus} mL at {initialRate} mL/hr

Subsequent: 70 mL/kg
= 70 x {weightKg} = {subsequentFluid} mL at {subsequentRate} mL/hr

Total 24h = {totalFluid} mL
(isInfant: < 12mo gets longer infusion times)
calculators.source: WHO. Cholera Outbreak Response Field Manual. 2019.

calculators.name: Lassa Fever Severity Triage
calculators.description: Clinical triage for Lassa fever severity. Guides admission and ribavirin decisions per NCDC/WHO criteria.

results.label: Severity score
results.formula: fever + bleeding + vomiting + soreThroat + facialSwelling + hypotension + confusion + creatinine

interpretations.label: Mild - outpatient monitoring if reliable follow-up
interpretations.detail: Supportive care. Report to NCDC.

interpretations.label: Moderate - admit for observation and supportive care
interpretations.detail: Isolate. Start ribavirin if within 6 days of onset. Monitor vitals.

interpretations.label: Severe - urgent admission, high-risk
interpretations.detail: Isolate immediately. Start ribavirin. Monitor for shock, renal failure. Notify NCDC.

calculators.source: Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC). National Guideline for Lassa Fever Case Management. 2018.

calculators.name: Snakebite Envenomation (20WBCT)
calculators.description: WHO-recommended 20-min whole blood clotting test for snakebite coagulopathy. Guides antivenom decision.

inputs.label: 20WBCT result (2 mL blood, glass tube, 20 min)

results.label: Envenomation score
results.formula: clotResult + localSwelling + systemicBleeding + ptosis + hypotension + oliguria

interpretations.label: Local envenomation - monitor systemic signs
interpretations.detail: Elevate limb. Monitor 20WBCT, urine output q4-6h.

interpretations.label: Systemic envenomation - give antivenom STAT
interpretations.detail: Give appropriate antivenom. Monitor for anaphylaxis. Repeat 20WBCT q6h.

calculators.source: WHO. Guidelines for the Management of Snake-bites. 2016. (20-minute whole blood clotting test)

calculators.name: Rabies PEP (Essen & Zagreb Regimens)
calculators.description: WHO post-exposure prophylaxis for rabies. Generates dosing schedule based on exposure category and regimen choice.

options.label: II - Nibble uncovered skin, minor scratch
III - Bite/scratch with bleeding, lick on broken skin

interpretations.label: Category II - Vaccine only
interpretations.detail: Thorough wound washing 15 min + vaccine per regimen.

interpretations.label: Category III - Vaccine + RIG
interpretations.detail: Wound washing + vaccine + RIG 20 IU/kg infiltrated around wound.

interpretations.label: Category III - Vaccine only (RIG unavailable)
interpretations.detail: Give vaccine. RIG can be given up to 7 days after first dose if available.

calculators.source: WHO. WHO Position on Rabies Immunization. 2018. (Essen + Zagreb regimens)

calculators.name: TB Treatment Phase Calculator
calculators.description: Determines TB treatment phase and duration per WHO/CDC guidelines for drug-susceptible and drug-resistant TB.
calculators.transparency_template:
TB Treatment:
Patient type: {patientType} (0=DS-pulmonary, 1=Extrapulm, 2=Retreat, 3=MDR)
HIV positive: {hivPositive}

Intensive phase: {intensivePhase} months
Total duration: {totalDuration} months
Completed: {monthsCompleted} months
Remaining: {monthsRemaining} months
Current phase: {phase} (0=Consult, 1=Intensive HRZE, 2=Continuation HR, 3=MDR-TB)

interpretations.label: Intensive phase - 4 drugs (HRZE)
interpretations.detail: Isoniazid + Rifampin + Pyrazinamide + Ethambutol. Ensure DOT.

interpretations.label: Continuation phase - 2 drugs (HR)
interpretations.detail: Isoniazid + Rifampin. Monitor for recurrence.

interpretations.label: MDR-TB - specialist referral
interpretations.detail: BPaLM regimen (6 months) or individualised longer regimen. Refer to TB specialist.

calculators.source: WHO. Treatment of Tuberculosis: Guidelines. 4th ed. 2010. (Plus 2022 updates)

calculators.name: Silverman-Anderson Score
calculators.description: Assesses neonatal respiratory distress severity. 5 signs scored 0-2 each, total 0-10. Higher score = worse distress.
calculators.transparency_template:
Silverman-Anderson Score (0-10):
Upper chest: {upperChest}/2
Lower chest retractions: {lowerChest}/2
Xiphoid retractions: {xiphoid}/2
Nasal flaring: {nasalFlaring}/2
Expiratory grunt: {expiratoryGrunting}/2
Total: {total}/10

interpretations.label: Mild respiratory distress
interpretations.detail: Observe. Consider nasal prongs O2 if desaturating.

interpretations.label: Moderate respiratory distress
interpretations.detail: Escalate care. Consider CPAP or nasal cannula. Monitor SpO2 and blood gas.

interpretations.label: Severe respiratory distress
interpretations.detail: Impending respiratory failure. Consider intubation and mechanical ventilation. Urgent NICU referral.

calculators.source: Silverman WA, Andersen DH. A controlled clinical trial of effects of water mist on obstructive respiratory signs, death rate and necropsy findings among premature infants. Pediatrics. 1956;17(1):1-10.

calculators.name: Downes-Vidyasagar Score (Neonatal RDS)
calculators.description: Assesses severity of neonatal respiratory distress syndrome. 5 parameters scored 0-2 each, total 0-10.
calculators.transparency_template:
Downes-Vidyasagar Score (0-10):
Respiratory rate: {respRate}/2
Cyanosis: {cyanosis}/2
Retractions: {retractions}/2
Expiratory grunt: {grunting}/2
Air entry: {airEntry}/2
Total: {total}/10

interpretations.label: Mild RDS - observe
interpretations.detail: Monitor vitals and SpO2. Consider nasal O2.

interpretations.label: Moderate RDS - escalate care
interpretations.detail: Consider CPAP, blood gas analysis, chest X-ray. NICU referral.

interpretations.label: Severe RDS - urgent intervention
interpretations.detail: Likely need intubation + surfactant + mechanical ventilation. Urgent NICU.

calculators.source: Downes JJ et al. Respiratory distress score for neonates. Anesth Analg. 1970;49(2):243-250.

calculators.name: Ballard Score (Newborn Gestational Age)
calculators.description: New Ballard Score assesses gestational age through 12 criteria (6 neuromuscular + 6 physical). Total score -10 to 50 maps to 20-44 weeks.

options.label: 1 - Elbow between midline and opposite AC
3 - Elbow between opposite AC and axillary line
0 - Lids open, pinna flat, stays folded
1 - Slightly curved pinna, soft, slow recoil
-1 - Male: scrotum flat/smooth; Female: clitoris prominent, labia flat
0 - Male: scrotum empty, faint rugae; Female: prominent clitoris, small minora
1 - Male: testes in upper canal, rare rugae; Female: prominent clitoris, enlarging minora
2 - Male: testes descending, few rugae; Female: majora and minora equally prominent
3 - Male: testes down, good rugae; Female: majora large, minora small
4 - Male: testes pendulous, deep rugae; Female: majora cover clitoris and minora

results.label: Neuromuscular total
results.formula: posture + squareWindow + armRecoil + poplitealAngle + scarfSign + heelToEar

results.label: Physical maturity total
results.formula: skin + lanugo + plantarSurface + breast + eyeEar + genitals

results.label: Total Ballard Score
results.formula: (posture + squareWindow + armRecoil + poplitealAngle + scarfSign + heelToEar) + (skin + lanugo + plantarSurface + breast + eyeEar + genitals)

results.label: Estimated gestational age
results.formula: ((posture + squareWindow + armRecoil + poplitealAngle + scarfSign + heelToEar) + (skin + lanugo + plantarSurface + breast + eyeEar + genitals) + 10) * 0.4 + 20

interpretations.label: Extreme preterm (<28 weeks)
interpretations.detail: NICU admission. Surfactant, thermal care, infection control.

interpretations.label: Very preterm (28-31 weeks)
interpretations.detail: NICU admission. Respiratory support, feeding support.

interpretations.label: Moderate preterm (32-33 weeks)
interpretations.detail: Special care nursery. Monitor temperature, feeding, jaundice.

interpretations.label: Late preterm (34-36 weeks)
interpretations.detail: Monitor for hypoglycaemia, jaundice, feeding difficulties.

interpretations.label: Term (37-42 weeks)
interpretations.detail: Mature newborn. Routine care.

interpretations.label: Post-term (>42 weeks)
interpretations.detail: Monitor for meconium aspiration, macrosomia, hypoglycaemia.

calculators.source: Ballard JL et al. New Ballard Score, expanded to include extremely premature infants. J Pediatr. 1991;119(3):417-423.

calculators.name: Kramer's Rule (Jaundice Progression)
calculators.description: Clinical assessment of neonatal jaundice by visual progression. Dermal staining follows cephalocaudal pattern. Guides need for bilirubin measurement.
calculators.transparency_template:
Kramer's Rule:
Highest jaundice zone: {jaundiceZone}/5

Estimated bilirubin range:
Zone 1 (Face): 4-6 mg/dL
Zone 2 (Upper trunk): 6-9 mg/dL
Zone 3 (Lower trunk): 9-12 mg/dL
Zone 4 (Arms/legs): 12-15 mg/dL
Zone 5 (Palms/soles): >15 mg/dL

Action: if zone >= 3, check serum bilirubin
If zone 2 + day < 3, check serum bilirubin
Otherwise monitor.

Note: Kramer's is a clinical screening tool.
Confirm with serum bilirubin before treatment decisions.

interpretations.label: Physiological jaundice - monitor
interpretations.detail: Zone 1 (face only). Typical for day 1-2. Monitor progression.

interpretations.label: Moderate - check bilirubin if <72h old
interpretations.detail: Zone 2. Estimated bilirubin 6-9 mg/dL. Check transcutaneous bilirubin.

interpretations.label: Zone 3 - check serum bilirubin
interpretations.detail: Estimated 9-12 mg/dL. Check TSB. Compare to Bhutani nomogram.

interpretations.label: Zone 4 - urgent bilirubin check
interpretations.detail: Estimated 12-15 mg/dL. Check TSB urgently. Assess need for phototherapy.

interpretations.label: Zone 5 - critical - emergency bilirubin
interpretations.detail: Estimated >15 mg/dL. Check TSB immediately. Prepare for phototherapy/exchange transfusion.

calculators.source: Kramer LI. Advancement of dermal icterus in the jaundiced newborn. Am J Dis Child. 1969;118(3):454-458.

calculators.name: Neonatal BP Norms
calculators.description: Calculates expected mean blood pressure (MAP) for neonates based on gestational age and postnatal days.
calculators.transparency_template:
Neonatal BP Norms:
GA: {gestationalAge} weeks | Day: {postnatalDays}

Expected MAP = GA + (Days x 0.5)
= {gestationalAge} + ({postnatalDays} x 0.5)
= {expectedMap} mmHg

Hypotension if MAP < {hypotensionThreshold} mmHg (gestational age)
Expected SBP: ~{normalSystolic} mmHg
Expected DBP: ~{normalDiastolic} mmHg

interpretations.label: HYPOTENSIVE - MAP below gestational age
interpretations.detail: MAP below GA in weeks. Assess volume status, consider inotrope support. NICU review.

interpretations.label: Low-normal MAP - monitor closely
interpretations.detail: MAP borderline. Recheck in 30 min. Ensure adequate perfusion.

calculators.source: Dionne JM et al. Hypertension in infancy: diagnosis, management and outcome. Pediatr Nephrol. 2012;27(1):17-32.

calculators.name: Neonatal Sepsis Screen
calculators.description: Evaluates neonatal sepsis risk using clinical risk factors and basic lab parameters. Not a substitute for clinical judgment or blood culture.

options.label: 1 - One risk factor (PROM, maternal fever, chorio)

calculators.transparency_template:
Neonatal Sepsis Screen:
Maternal risk factors: {riskFactors}/2
Clinical signs: {clinicalSigns}/2
CRP >10 mg/L: {crpPoint}/1 | I:T >0.2: {itRatioPoint}/1 | WBC abnormal: {wbcPoint}/1
Total: {total}/7

interpretations.label: Low risk - observe
interpretations.detail: Low probability of sepsis. Monitor vitals and clinical status. Reassess if deteriorates.

interpretations.label: Moderate risk - consider empiric antibiotics
interpretations.detail: Obtain blood culture. Start empiric antibiotics (Ampicillin + Gentamicin). Monitor.

interpretations.label: High risk - start empiric antibiotics STAT
interpretations.detail: Obtain blood culture, LP if stable. Start empiric antibiotics immediately. NICU admission.

calculators.source: Rodwell RL et al. Early diagnosis of neonatal sepsis using a hematologic scoring system. J Pediatr. 1988;112(5):761-767.

calculators.name: CHA₂DS₂-VASc Score (AFib Stroke Risk)
calculators.description: Estimates annual stroke risk in non-valvular atrial fibrillation. Used to guide anticoagulation decisions.

inputs.label: Vascular disease (PAD, prior MI, aortic plaque)

results.label: CHA₂DS₂-VASc Score
results.formula: chf + hypertension + age + diabetes + stroke + vascular + femaleSex

interpretations.label: Low risk: annual stroke risk ~0.9%
interpretations.detail: No anticoagulation recommended (CHA₂DS₂-VASc = 0 in males).

interpretations.label: Low risk: annual stroke risk ~1.2%
interpretations.detail: No anticoagulation recommended (score driven by female sex alone).

interpretations.label: Moderate risk: annual stroke risk ~1.7-4.0%
interpretations.detail: Consider anticoagulation weighing bleeding risk (HAS-BLED). Discuss shared decision-making.

interpretations.label: High risk: annual stroke risk ≥4.6%
interpretations.detail: Recommmend anticoagulation (DOAC or warfarin) unless contraindicated. Assess HAS-BLED.

calculators.source: Lip GYH et al. Refining clinical risk stratification for predicting stroke in atrial fibrillation using a novel risk factor-based approach (CHA2DS2-VASc). Chest. 2010;137(2):263-272.

calculators.name: HAS-BLED Score (Bleeding Risk)
calculators.description: Estimates 1-year major bleeding risk in patients on anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation.

inputs.label: Abnormal renal function (Cr >2.26 mg/dL or dialysis)
Abnormal liver function (cirrhosis or ALT/AST >3× ULN)

results.label: HAS-BLED Score
results.formula: hbp + abnormalRenal + abnormalLiver + strokeHistory + bleeding + labileInr + elderly + drugsAlcohol

interpretations.label: Low bleeding risk: 1-year major bleed ~2%
interpretations.detail: HAS-BLED ≤2. Bleeding risk acceptable for anticoagulation.

interpretations.label: Moderate bleeding risk: 1-year major bleed ~4-6%
interpretations.detail: HAS-BLED ≥3 indicates high risk. Address modifiable risk factors. Re-evaluate anticoagulation need.

interpretations.label: High bleeding risk: 1-year major bleed ≥8%
interpretations.detail: Very high bleeding risk. Exercise extreme caution. Correct modifiable factors. Consider LAA occlusion vs NOAC with caution.

calculators.source: Pisters R et al. A novel user-friendly score (HAS-BLED) to assess 1-year risk of major bleeding in patients with atrial fibrillation. Chest. 2010;138(5):1093-1100.

calculators.name: CKD Staging by eGFR & ACR
calculators.description: Stages chronic kidney disease based on eGFR (G-category) and albumin-to-creatinine ratio (A-category) per KDIGO 2024 guidelines.
calculators.transparency_template:
CKD Staging (KDIGO 2024):
eGFR: {egfr} mL/min/1.73m²
ACR: {acr} mg/g

See interpretation for combined G + A stage.

Risk: G1-2+A1 low | G1-2+A2 mod | G3a+A1 mod
G3a+A2 high | G3b+A1 high | G1-2+A3 high
G3b+A2 very high | G3a-b+A3 very high
G4-5: very high risk

interpretations.label: Stage G3a
interpretations.detail: Monitor eGFR and ACR annually. Manage CV risk. Review medications.

interpretations.label: Stage G3b
interpretations.detail: Monitor eGFR and ACR every 6 months. Consider nephrology referral.

interpretations.label: Stage G4 — Severe
interpretations.detail: Nephrology referral. Prepare for RRT. Monitor Hb, Ca/PO4, PTH, bicarbonate.

interpretations.label: Stage G5 — Kidney Failure
interpretations.detail: RRT (dialysis or transplant) evaluation urgent. Access planning.

calculators.source: KDIGO 2012 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of CKD. Kidney Int Suppl. 2013;3(1):1-150.

calculators.name: Adjusted Body Weight (Drug Dosing)
calculators.description: Calculates Ideal Body Weight (IBW) and Adjusted Body Weight (AdjBW) for drug dosing in patients with obesity. AdjBW = IBW + 0.4 × (Actual − IBW).
calculators.transparency_template:
Adjusted Body Weight (AdjBW):

IBW (Devine) = 2.3 × (height(in) − 60) + {baseWt}
= 2.3 × ({heightCm} / 2.54 − 60) + {baseWt}
= {ibw} kg

AdjBW = IBW + 0.4 × (Actual − IBW)
= {ibw} + 0.4 × ({weightKg} − {ibw})
= {adjbw} kg

interpretations.label: Weight ≤120% IBW — use actual weight for dosing
Overweight — consider AdjBW for hydrophilic drugs
interpretations.detail: AdjBW: {adjbw} kg. Use AdjBW for aminoglycosides, vancomycin, heparin.

interpretations.label: Obese — use AdjBW for most hydrophilic drugs
interpretations.detail: AdjBW: {adjbw} kg. Consider TBW for lipophilic drugs (propofol, benzodiazepines).

calculators.source: Bauer LA. Applied Clinical Pharmacokinetics. McGraw-Hill. 2001. (Adjustment for obesity dosing)

calculators.name: BODE Index (COPD Prognosis)
calculators.description: Multi-dimensional index predicting mortality in COPD. Combines Body mass index, Obstruction, Dyspnoea, and Exercise capacity.

options.label: 2 - Walks slower than peers due to SOB
3 - Stops after walking 100m or few minutes
4 - Too breathless to leave house / breathless dressing

calculators.transparency_template:
BODE Index:

BMI ≤21: {bmiPoints}/1
FEV1%: {fev1Percent}/3
mMRC: {mmrc}/4
6MWD: {sixMinWalk}/3

Total: {total}/10

interpretations.label: Mild — 4-year survival ~80%
interpretations.detail: BODE 0-2. Low mortality risk. Continue standard COPD management.

interpretations.label: Moderate — 4-year survival ~67%
interpretations.detail: BODE 3-4. Consider pulmonary rehabilitation. Optimise inhalers.

interpretations.label: Severe — 4-year survival ~57%
interpretations.detail: BODE 5-6. Assess for LTOT, pulmonary rehab, lung volume reduction. Involve respiratory specialist.

interpretations.label: Very severe — 4-year survival ~18%
interpretations.detail: BODE 7-10. High mortality. Assess for lung transplant, palliative care. Optimise all therapies.

calculators.source: Celli BR et al. The body-mass index, airflow obstruction, dyspnea, and exercise capacity index in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. N Engl J Med. 2004;350(10):1005-1012.

calculators.name: BISAP Score (Pancreatitis Mortality)
calculators.description: Bedside Index of Severity in Acute Pancreatitis. Predicts in-hospital mortality within 24 hours of presentation.

interpretations.label: Mortality ~0.5% — Low risk
interpretations.detail: Supportive care. Monitor for deterioration.

interpretations.label: Mortality ~2.0% — Moderate risk
interpretations.detail: Admit for monitoring. Consider ICU if any organ failure.

interpretations.label: Mortality ~8.0% — High risk
interpretations.detail: ICU admission recommended. Aggressive fluid resuscitation. Monitor for organ failure.

interpretations.label: Mortality ~20% — Severe risk
interpretations.detail: ICU care. Multi-organ failure likely.

interpretations.label: Mortality >40% — Critical
interpretations.detail: ICU care. High probability of death. Multi-disciplinary team involvement.

calculators.source: Wu BU et al. The early prediction of mortality in acute pancreatitis: a large population-based study. Gut. 2008;57(12):1698-1703.

calculators.name: Free Water Deficit (Hypernatraemia)
calculators.description: Calculates the total body water deficit in hypernatraemia. Helps guide fluid replacement therapy.
calculators.transparency_template:
Free Water Deficit:
TBW = {genderLabel} factor × {weightKg} kg

Deficit = TBW × (1 − 140/Na)
= TBW × (1 − 140/{currentNa})
= {freeWaterDeficit} L

Correct 50% over 24h: {halfDeficit} L

interpretations.label: Mild deficit — monitor
interpretations.detail: Correct with oral/IV hypotonic fluids. Recheck Na in 6h.

interpretations.label: Moderate deficit
interpretations.detail: Replace deficit with D5W or 1/2 NS. Correct 0.5 mmol/L/h max. Recheck Na q4-6h.

interpretations.label: Severe deficit — cautious correction
interpretations.detail: Correct slowly — max 8-12 mmol/L in 24h. Use D5W. Frequent Na monitoring. ICU-level care recommended.

calculators.source: Adrogue HJ, Madias NE. Hypernatremia. N Engl J Med. 2000;342(20):1493-1499.

calculators.name: Hypertonic (3%) Saline Dose (Hyponatraemia)
calculators.description: Calculates the volume of 3% hypertonic saline needed to achieve a desired rise in serum sodium for severe symptomatic hyponatraemia.
calculators.transparency_template:
3% Hypertonic Saline Dose:
[Na in 3% NS = 513 mEq/L]

Volume = ΔNa × TBW / 0.513
= {targetDeltaNa} × (sex factor × {weightKg2}) × 1000 / 513
= {volume3pct} mL

Expected Na: {expectedNa} mmol/L

Guidelines: max 4-6 mEq rise in 24h if asymptomatic, 8-10 if seizing.

interpretations.label: Conservative rise — recommended for asymptomatic patients
Aggressive rise — for active seizures only
interpretations.detail: Give as IV bolus over 10 min. May repeat once if seizures continue. Stop rise after 10 mEq total in 24h.

interpretations.label: Excessive — risk of osmotic demyelination
interpretations.detail: Max recommended rise: 10 mEq/L in first 24h. Reduce target rise.

calculators.source: Verbalis JG et al. Diagnosis, evaluation, and treatment of hyponatremia. Am J Med. 2013;126(10 Suppl 1):S1-S42.

calculators.name: Tidal Volume (ARDSNet / PBW)
calculators.description: Calculates predicted body weight (PBW) and lung-protective tidal volume (6 mL/kg and 8 mL/kg) per ARDSNet protocol.
calculators.transparency_template:
ARDSNet Lung-Protective Ventilation:

PBW ({genderLabel}) = {baseWt} + 0.91 × (height − 152.4)
= {pbw} kg

VT 6 mL/kg = 6 × {pbw} = {tv6mL} mL ({tv6} L)
VT 8 mL/kg = 8 × {pbw} = (computed) mL ({tv8} L)

interpretations.label: Initial vent settings
interpretations.detail: Set Vt = {tv6mL} mL ({tv6} L). Target plateau pressure <30 cmH2O. Adjust RR to maintain pH >7.2.

calculators.source: ARDS Network. Ventilation with lower tidal volumes as compared with traditional tidal volumes for acute lung injury and the ARDS. N Engl J Med. 2000;342(18):1301-1308.

calculators.name: ABCD² Score (TIA Stroke Risk)
calculators.description: Stratifies 2-day stroke risk after transient ischaemic attack (TIA). Guides urgent assessment and admission decisions.

interpretations.label: Low risk: 2-day stroke risk ~1%
interpretations.detail: Consider urgent outpatient stroke clinic assessment within 1 week.

interpretations.label: Moderate risk: 2-day stroke risk ~4%
interpretations.detail: Admit for observation, urgent CTA/MRA, cardiac monitoring, and stroke prevention.

interpretations.label: High risk: 2-day stroke risk ~8%
interpretations.detail: Admit urgently. Immediate neuroimaging, cardiac monitoring, antiplatelet therapy, vascular assessment.

calculators.source: Johnston SC et al. Validation and refinement of scores to predict very early stroke risk after TIA. Lancet. 2007;369(9558):283-292.

calculators.name: Bishop Score (Cervical Ripeness)
calculators.description: Assesses cervical favourability for induction of labour. Score ≥6 indicates favourable cervix and higher likelihood of successful vaginal delivery.
calculators.transparency_template:
Bishop Score (0-13):
Dilation: {dilation}/3
Effacement: {effacement}/3
Station: {station}/3
Consistency: {consistency}/2
Position: {position}/2
Total: {total}/13

interpretations.label: Unfavourable — low likelihood of successful induction
interpretations.detail: Consider cervical ripening agent (PGE1/PGE2) before induction. Higher risk of failed IOL and Caesarean.

interpretations.label: Intermediate — consider ripening
interpretations.detail: May benefit from cervical ripening. Reassess after ripening.

interpretations.label: Favourable — good candidate for induction
interpretations.detail: Bishop ≥6. Favourable cervix. High likelihood of successful vaginal delivery with induction.

interpretations.label: Very favourable — high success
interpretations.detail: Very favourable cervix. Proceed with induction as indicated.

calculators.source: Bishop EH. Pelvic scoring for elective induction. Obstet Gynecol. 1964;24:266-268.

calculators.name: HOMA-IR (Insulin Resistance)
calculators.description: Homeostatic Model Assessment of Insulin Resistance. Estimates insulin resistance from fasting glucose and insulin levels.

interpretations.label: Mild insulin resistance
interpretations.detail: HOMA-IR ≥2.5 suggests insulin resistance. Consider lifestyle modification. Assess for metabolic syndrome.

interpretations.label: Moderate insulin resistance
interpretations.detail: High risk for type 2 diabetes and metabolic syndrome. Measure HbA1c, fasting lipids. Lifestyle + pharmacotherapy if indicated.

interpretations.label: Severe insulin resistance
interpretations.detail: Very high insulin resistance. Check for type 2 diabetes, PCOS, NAFLD, metabolic syndrome. Refer to endocrinology.

calculators.source: Matthews DR et al. Homeostasis model assessment: insulin resistance and beta-cell function from fasting plasma glucose and insulin concentrations in man. Diabetologia. 1985;28(7):412-419.

calculators.name: Lactate Clearance (Sepsis)
calculators.description: Percentage change in serum lactate after initial resuscitation. Lower lactate clearance (<10%) is associated with worse outcomes in sepsis.
calculators.transparency_template:
Lactate Clearance = (Initial − Repeat) / Initial × 100%
= ({initialLactate} − {repeatLactate}) / {initialLactate} × 100%
= {clearancePct}%

interpretations.label: Good clearance — adequate resuscitation
interpretations.detail: Lactate clearance ≥20% associated with improved survival. Continue management.

interpretations.label: Moderate clearance — re-assess
interpretations.detail: Lactate clearance 10-20%. Re-assess volume status and tissue perfusion. Consider repeat lactate in 1-2h.

interpretations.label: Poor clearance — <10%, escalate care
interpretations.detail: Lactate clearance <10% associated with worse outcomes. Re-assess resuscitation. Consider vasopressors/inotropes. Escalate to ICU.

interpretations.label: Worsening lactate — clinical deterioration
interpretations.detail: Lactate rising. Ongoing hypoperfusion or new source of sepsis. Immediate reassessment of ABCDE. Escalate care emergently.

calculators.source: Nguyen HB et al. Early lactate clearance is associated with improved outcome in severe sepsis and septic shock. Crit Care Med. 2004;32(8):1637-1642.

calculators.name: Waist-to-Hip Ratio (WHR)
calculators.description: Simple measure of central obesity. Ratio of waist circumference to hip circumference. Predicts cardiovascular and metabolic risk.

interpretations.label: High risk — central obesity (male ≥0.90)
interpretations.detail: WHR ≥0.90 in males. Increased risk of CVD, type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome. Recommend lifestyle intervention.

interpretations.label: High risk — central obesity (female ≥0.85)
interpretations.detail: WHR ≥0.85 in females. Increased risk of CVD, type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome. Recommend lifestyle intervention.

calculators.source: WHO. Waist circumference and waist-hip ratio: report of a WHO expert consultation. 2008.

calculators.name: Lean Body Mass (Boer Formula)
calculators.description: Estimates lean body mass (LBM) using the Boer formula. Useful for drug dosing, nutritional assessment, and metabolic studies.
calculators.transparency_template:
Lean Body Mass (Boer 1984):

Male: 0.407 × W + 0.267 × H − 19.2
Female: 0.252 × W + 0.473 × H − 48.3
= {lbm} kg

Fat mass: {fatMass} kg ({fatPct}%)

interpretations.label: Very low body fat — athlete range
Obese range — increased metabolic/cardiovascular risk
interpretations.detail: Body fat ≥30%. Strongly recommend weight management, dietary counselling, exercise programme.

calculators.source: Boer P. Estimated lean body mass as an index for normalization of body fluid volumes in humans. Am J Physiol. 1984;247(4 Pt 2):F632-636.

calculators.name: Absolute Neutrophil Count (ANC)
calculators.description: Calculates ANC from WBC, neutrophil percentage, and band percentage. Essential for assessing neutropenia and infection risk.
calculators.transparency_template:
ANC = WBC × (Neutrophils + Bands) / 100
= {wbcAnc} × ({neutrophils} + {bands}) / 100
= {anc} x10⁹/L

interpretations.label: Normal — low infection risk
interpretations.detail: ANC ≥2.0. Normal neutrophil count. Routine monitoring.

interpretations.label: Mild neutropenia — minimal risk
interpretations.detail: ANC 1.5-2.0. Monitor. No specific precautions needed.

interpretations.label: Mild neutropenia
interpretations.detail: ANC 1.0-1.5. Caution with myelosuppressive drugs. No increased infection risk at rest.

interpretations.label: Moderate neutropenia
interpretations.detail: ANC 0.5-1.0. Increased infection risk. Consider growth factor (G-CSF) if chemotherapy-related.

interpretations.label: Severe neutropenia — febrile neutropenia risk
interpretations.detail: ANC <0.5. High risk of life-threatening infection. Strict hand hygiene, neutropenic precautions. Urgent evaluation if febrile. Consider prophylactic antibiotics and G-CSF.

calculators.source: National Cancer Institute. Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE). 2017.

calculators.name: Waist-to-Height Ratio (WHtR)
calculators.description: Simple central obesity measure. WHtR <0.5 considered healthy. More sensitive than BMI for cardiometabolic risk.
calculators.transparency_template:
WHtR = Waist / Height = {whrWaist} / {whrHeight} = {whtr}

Healthy: <0.5
Increased risk: ≥0.5
High risk: ≥0.6

interpretations.label: Increased risk — central adiposity
interpretations.detail: WHtR ≥0.5. Increased risk of CVD, diabetes, metabolic syndrome.

interpretations.label: High risk — substantial central obesity
interpretations.detail: WHtR ≥0.6. Very high cardiometabolic risk. Urgent lifestyle intervention.

calculators.source: Ashwell M, Gibson S. Waist-to-height ratio as an indicator of early childhood health. Nutr Rev. 2016;74(9):538-546.

calculators.name: Brooke Formula (Burns)
calculators.description: Alternative to Parkland for burn fluid resuscitation. Uses 1.5 mL/kg/%BSA crystalloid + 0.5 mL/kg colloid + 2000 mL D5W.
calculators.transparency_template:
Brooke Formula (24h):
Crystalloid: 1.5 mL × {brookeWt} × {brookeBsa} = {brooke24hCrystalloid} mL
Colloid: 0.5 mL × {brookeWt} × {brookeBsa} = {brooke24hColloid} mL
D5W: 2000 mL

Half in first 8h, half in next 16h.
calculators.source: Reiss E et al. Fluid and electrolyte balance in burns. JAMA. 1953;152(14):1309-1313.

calculators.name: Modified Brooke Formula (Burns)
calculators.description: Updated Brooke formula: 2 mL/kg/%BSA RL in 24h. No colloid in first 24h.
calculators.transparency_template:
Modified Brooke (24h):
RL = 2 mL × {mbWt} × {mbBsa} = {mbTotal} mL
Half in first 8h, rest over 16h.
No colloid in first 24h per protocol.
calculators.source: American Burn Association. Practice Guidelines for Burn Care. J Burn Care Rehabil. 2001.

calculators.name: Sodium Deficit (Hyponatraemia)
calculators.description: Calculates sodium deficit for correcting hyponatraemia. TBW = 0.6 × wt (M) or 0.5 × wt (F).
calculators.transparency_template:
Na Deficit = (Target − Current) × TBW
= ({naDeficitTarget} − {naDeficitCurrent}) × ({naDeficitSex ? 0.6 : 0.5} × {naDeficitWt})
= {sodiumDeficit} mmol

Correct max 6-8 mmol/L in 24h. 0.5 mmol/L/h rate limit.
calculators.source: Adrogue HJ, Madias NE. Hyponatremia. N Engl J Med. 2000;342(21):1581-1589.

calculators.name: Potassium Replacement (IV/Oral)
calculators.description: Estimates potassium deficit. K deficit (mmol) = (target K - current K) × weight × 0.4.

results.label: Approx oral KCl tablets (8 mmol each)

calculators.transparency_template:
K Deficit = (Target − Current) × Wt × 0.4
= ({kTarget} − {kCurrent}) × {kWt} × 0.4
= {kDeficit} mmol

Max IV rate: 10-20 mmol/h peripheral, 40 mmol/h central.
÷ 8 mmol/tab = {kOral} tablets.
calculators.source: Cohn JN et al. New guidelines for potassium replacement in clinical practice. Arch Intern Med. 2000;160(16):2429-2436.

calculators.name: Fluid Balance (24h I/O)
calculators.description: Calculates net 24-hour fluid balance from total input and output.
calculators.transparency_template:
24h Fluid Balance:
Input: IV {ivFluids} + Oral {oralIntake} = {totalInput} mL
Output: Urine {urineOutput} + Other {otherLosses} = {totalOutput} mL
Net: {totalInput} − {totalOutput} = {netBalance} mL
calculators.source: Bennett JE, Plum F. Cecil Textbook of Medicine. 25th ed. Saunders. 1996.

calculators.name: eGFR (MDRD)
calculators.description: MDRD Study equation for eGFR. Less accurate than CKD-EPI at higher GFR.
calculators.transparency_template:
MDRD eGFR = 175 × Cr⁻¹·¹⁵⁴ × Age⁻⁰·²⁰³ × {mdrdFemale == 1 ? "× 0.742" : ""} {mdrdBlack == 1 ? "× 1.212" : ""}
= {mdrdEgfr} mL/min/1.73m²
calculators.source: Levey AS et al. A more accurate method to estimate glomerular filtration rate from serum creatinine: a new prediction equation. Ann Intern Med. 1999;130(6):461-470.

calculators.name: eGFR (CKD-EPI 2021 Race-free)
calculators.description: CKD-EPI 2021 equation without race coefficient. Preferred for accurate GFR estimation.

results.label: eGFR (CKD-EPI 2021)
results.formula: (ckdFemale == 1 ? (ckdCr <= 0.7 ? 142 * pow(ckdCr / 0.7, -0.241) * pow(0.9938, ckdAge) : 142 * pow(ckdCr / 0.7, -1.2) * pow(0.9938, ckdAge)) : (ckdCr <= 0.9 ? 142 * pow(ckdCr / 0.9, -0.302) * pow(0.9938, ckdAge) : 142 * pow(ckdCr / 0.9, -1.2) * pow(0.9938, ckdAge)))

calculators.transparency_template:
CKD-EPI 2021 (Race-free):
Cystatin C not used.
eGFR = {ckdepi} mL/min/1.73m²
calculators.source: Inker LA et al. New creatinine- and cystatin C-based equations to estimate GFR without race. N Engl J Med. 2021;385(19):1737-1749.

calculators.name: Urea Reduction Ratio (URR)
calculators.description: Measures haemodialysis adequacy. URR = (pre-urea - post-urea) / pre-urea × 100%. Target >65%.
calculators.transparency_template:
URR = (Pre − Post) / Pre × 100%
= ({preUrea} − {postUrea}) / {preUrea} × 100%
= {urr}%
Target: >65% for adequate dialysis
calculators.source: Lowrie EG et al. The urea reduction ratio (URR) and KT/V are both valid measures of dialysis adequacy. Semin Dial. 1990;3(2):82-87.

calculators.name: Kt/V (Dialysis Adequacy)
calculators.description: Daugirdas formula for haemodialysis adequacy. Kt/V ≥1.2 is target for thrice-weekly HD.
calculators.transparency_template:
Daugirdas Kt/V:
= −ln(R − 0.008 × t) + (4 − 3.5R) × UF/W
= {ktv}

Target: ≥1.2 (thrice-weekly), ≥1.4 (twice-weekly)
calculators.source: Daugirdas JT. Second generation logarithmic estimates of single-pool variable volume Kt/V. J Am Soc Nephrol. 1993;4(5):1205-1213.

calculators.name: FeUrea (Fractional Excretion of Urea)
calculators.description: Distinguishes prerenal from intrinsic AKI in patients on diuretics. FeUrea <35% suggests prerenal cause.
calculators.transparency_template:
FeUrea = (Uurea × SCr) / (SUN × UCr) × 100%
= {feUrea}%

<35%: Prerenal AKI
>50%: Intrinsic AKI (ATN)

interpretations.label: Prerenal AKI — likely volume responsive
interpretations.detail: FeUrea <35%. Consider fluid challenge. Evaluate volume status.

interpretations.label: Intrinsic AKI (ATN) — unlikely volume responsive
interpretations.detail: FeUrea >50%. Suggests established ATN. Avoid excessive fluids. Consider renal consult.

calculators.source: Carvounis CP et al. A urinary fractional excretion of urea calculation can distinguish pre-renal AKI from ATN in patients with diuretic use. Kidney Int. 2002;62(6):2223-2229.

calculators.name: Trans-Tubular Potassium Gradient (TTKG)
calculators.description: Assesses renal potassium handling. Normally <4 in hyperkalaemia (appropriate response). >8 in hypoK suggests renal K wasting.
calculators.transparency_template:
TTKG = (UrineK / (Uosm / Posm)) / SerumK
= {ttkg}

HyperK with TTKG <5: poor K secretion
HypoK with TTKG >4: renal K wasting

interpretations.label: Inadequate K secretion — consider mineralocorticoid deficiency
Excessive K wasting — check for hyperaldosteronism

calculators.source: Ethier JH et al. The transtubular potassium concentration gradient in patients with hypokalemia. Clin Nephrol. 1990;34(1):23-28.

calculators.name: Urine Anion Gap (RTA)
calculators.description: Differentiates types of metabolic acidosis. UAG = Na + K - Cl. Negative gap suggests GI loss. Positive gap suggests RTA.
calculators.transparency_template:
UAG = Na + K − Cl
= {uNa} + {uK} − {uCl}
= {urineAG} mmol/L

Negative: GI bicarbonate loss (diarrhoea)
Positive: Renal tubular acidosis (RTA)
calculators.source: Battle DC et al. The urine anion gap is a useful adjunct in distinguishing renal vs extrarenal causes of metabolic acidosis. Clin Nephrol. 1988;30(3):149-154.

calculators.name: Bladder Capacity (Paediatric)
calculators.description: Estimated bladder capacity for children: 30 + (age in years × 30) mL.
calculators.transparency_template:
Bladder Capacity = 30 + (Age × 30)
= 30 + ({childAgeBladder} × 30)
= {bladderCap} mL

Normal capacity for age.
calculators.source: Koff SA. Estimating bladder capacity in children. Urology. 1983;21(3):248.

calculators.name: ASCVD 10-Year Risk (Pooled Cohort)
calculators.description: AHA/ACC 2013 pooled cohort equations estimate 10-year atherosclerotic CVD risk.

results.label: 10-year ASCVD risk
results.formula: ascvdAge + ascvdTc + ascvdHdl + ascvdSbp + ascvdTreated + ascvdDiabetes + ascvdSmoker + ascvdMale + ascvdBlack

calculators.transparency_template:
ASCVD Pooled Cohort Risk:
Risk factors considered: age, sex, race, cholesterol, BP, DM, smoking
See AHA/ACC guidelines for full equation.
Simplified score: {ascvdRisk}

interpretations.label: Borderline risk (5-7.5%)
interpretations.detail: Consider statin if risk-enhancing factors present.

interpretations.label: Intermediate risk (7.5-20%)
interpretations.detail: Moderate-intensity statin recommended. Discuss with patient.

interpretations.label: High risk (≥20%)
interpretations.detail: High-intensity statin recommended. Aggressive risk factor management.

calculators.source: Goff DC Jr et al. 2013 ACC/AHA Guideline on the Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk. Circulation. 2014;129(25 Suppl 2):S49-73.

calculators.name: HEART Score (Chest Pain)
calculators.description: 5-component score predicts major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in undifferentiated chest pain. Each 0-2.

inputs.label: Risk factors (DM, smoker, HTN, dyslip, FH, obesity)

interpretations.label: Low risk — MACE ~1.7%
interpretations.detail: Consider discharge with early outpatient follow-up.

interpretations.label: Moderate risk — MACE ~16%
interpretations.detail: Admit for observation, serial troponins, functional testing.

interpretations.label: High risk — MACE ~50%
interpretations.detail: Urgent cardiology consult. Consider early invasive strategy.

calculators.source: Six AJ, Backus BE, Kelder JC. Chest pain in the emergency room: value of the HEART score. Neth Heart J. 2008;16(6):191-196.

calculators.name: TIMI Risk Score (STEMI)
calculators.description: Predicts 30-day mortality in STEMI patients. 0-14 based on 8 variables.

results.label: TIMI STEMI Score
results.formula: timisAge + timisAge65 + timisDiabetes + timisSbp + timisHr + timisKillip + timisWeight + timisSt + timisTime

calculators.source: Morrow DA et al. TIMI risk score for ST-elevation myocardial infarction: A convenient, bedside, clinical score for risk assessment at presentation. Circulation. 2000;102(17):2031-2037.

calculators.name: TIMI Risk Score (NSTEMI/UA)
calculators.description: Predicts 14-day composite endpoint (death, MI, urgent revasc) in NSTEMI/UA. 7 variables.

inputs.label: ≥3 CAD risk factors (FH, HTN, DM, smoker, dyslip)

results.label: TIMI NSTEMI/UA Score
results.formula: timinsAge + timinsRisk + timinsCad + timinsAspirin + timinsAngina + timinsSt + timinsTrop

calculators.source: Antman EM et al. The TIMI risk score for unstable angina/non-ST elevation MI: A method for prognostication and therapeutic decision making. JAMA. 2000;284(7):835-842.

calculators.name: Wells Score for DVT
calculators.description: Clinical prediction rule for deep vein thrombosis. 2-tier or 3-tier probability scoring.

inputs.label: Paralysis, paresis, or recent plaster immobilisation of leg
Bedridden >3 days or major surgery within 12 weeks
Localised tenderness along deep venous system
Calf swelling >3 cm compared to asymptomatic leg
Pitting oedema confined to symptomatic leg
Alternative diagnosis as likely or more likely than DVT

results.label: Wells DVT Score
results.formula: dvtActiveCa + dvtParalysis + dvtBedrest + dvtTender + dvtSwollen + dvtCalf + dvtPitting + dvtCollaterals + dvtPriorDvt + dvtAlternative

interpretations.label: DVT likely — proceed to ultrasound
interpretations.detail: Wells ≥2. DVT likely. Order compression ultrasound within 24h.

interpretations.label: DVT unlikely — check D-dimer
interpretations.detail: Wells <2. If D-dimer negative, DVT ruled out. If positive, order ultrasound.

calculators.source: Wells PS et al. Value of assessment of pretest probability of DVT in clinical management. Lancet. 1997;350(9094):1795-1798.

calculators.name: Revised Geneva Score (PE)
calculators.description: Clinical prediction rule for pulmonary embolism. Validated alternative to Wells for PE.

inputs.label: Clinical signs of DVT (pain on palpation, unilateral oedema)

results.label: Revised Geneva Score
results.formula: genAge + genPriorDVT + genSurgery + genActiveCa + genHr + genHr95 + genHemoptysis + genPainLeg + genSignsDvt

interpretations.label: Low probability — PE ~8%
interpretations.detail: Low clinical probability. Check D-dimer. If negative, PE excluded.

interpretations.label: High probability — PE ~74%
interpretations.detail: High clinical probability. Proceed to CTPA directly.

calculators.source: Le Gal G et al. Prediction of pulmonary embolism in the emergency department: the revised Geneva score. Ann Intern Med. 2006;144(3):165-171.

calculators.name: Age-Adjusted D-Dimer Cutoff
calculators.description: Adjusts D-dimer cutoff for age: for patients >50 years, cutoff = age × 0.1 mg/L FEU.
calculators.transparency_template:
Age-Adjusted D-Dimer:
<50 years: 0.5 mg/L cutoff
≥50 years: Age × 0.1 = {ddimerCutoff} mg/L cutoff

D-dimer = {ddimerValue} mg/L
Result: {ddimerInterpretation == 0 ? "Negative" : "Positive"}
calculators.source: Righini M et al. Age-adjusted D-dimer cutoff levels to rule out pulmonary embolism: a prospective outcome study. JAMA. 2014;311(11):1117-1124.

calculators.name: GOLD Classification (COPD)
calculators.description: GOLD 2024 classification for COPD severity and management groups (A, B, E).

interpretations.label: Group A — minimal symptoms, low exacerbation risk
interpretations.detail: LAMA or LAMA/LABA for symptom relief. Annual influenza/pneumococcal vax.

interpretations.label: Group B — more symptoms, low exacerbation risk
interpretations.detail: LAMA/LABA. Consider ICS if blood eosinophils ≥300.

interpretations.label: Group E — high exacerbation risk
interpretations.detail: LAMA/LABA + ICS if eos ≥100. Pulmonary rehab. Treat exacerbations aggressively.

calculators.source: GOLD. Global Strategy for the Diagnosis, Management, and Prevention of COPD. 2024 Report.

calculators.name: Oxygen Delivery (DO2)
calculators.description: DO2 = Cardiac Output × CaO2 × 10. Normal ~1000 mL/min. Assesses adequacy of tissue oxygen delivery.
calculators.transparency_template:
DO2 = CO × (1.34 × Hb × SaO2 + 0.003 × PaO2) × 10
≈ {do2} mL/min
Normal: ~900-1100 mL/min
Critical level: <350-400 mL/min
calculators.source: Fournier L et al. Oxygen delivery: a primary goal in critical care medicine. Crit Care. 2001;5(5):245-251.

calculators.name: Oxygen Consumption (VO2)
calculators.description: VO2 = CO × (CaO2 - CvO2) × 10. Normal ~250 mL/min. Fick principle.
calculators.transparency_template:
VO2 = CO × [1.34 × Hb × (SaO2 − SvO2)] × 10
= {vo2} mL/min
Normal: ~200-280 mL/min
Low: inadequate tissue demand/metabolism
calculators.source: Levy MN. Oxygen consumption and the heart. Annu Rev Physiol. 1979;41:85-94.

calculators.name: Alveolar Gas Equation (PAO2)
calculators.description: PAO2 = (FiO2 × 713) − (PaCO2 / 0.8). Used to calculate A-a gradient and assess gas exchange.
calculators.transparency_template:
PAO2 = FiO2 × (Patm − PH2O) − PaCO2 / RQ
= {fio2Alv} × (760 − 47) − {paco2Alv} / 0.8
= {pao2Alv} mmHg

Normal on room air: ~90-110 mmHg
A-a gradient = {pao2Alv} − PaO2 (measured)
Normal A-a: <10-15 (age/4 + 4)
calculators.source: Fenn WO, Rahn H, Otis AB. A theoretical study of the composition of the alveolar air at altitude. Am J Physiol. 1946;146:637-653.

calculators.name: Shunt Fraction (Qs/Qt)
calculators.description: Qs/Qt estimates the fraction of cardiac output bypassing gas exchange. Normal <5%. >20% severe.
calculators.transparency_template:
Qs/Qt = (CcO2 − CaO2) / (CcO2 − CvO2) × 100%
= ({ccO2} − {caO2}) / ({ccO2} − {cvO2}) × 100%
= {qsQt}%

Normal: <5% | Significant: >10% | Severe: >20%

interpretations.label: Severe shunt — refractory hypoxaemia
interpretations.detail: Qs/Qt >20%. Severe gas exchange impairment. Consider prone positioning, ECMO referral.

calculators.source: West JB. Pulmonary Pathophysiology — The Essentials. 8th ed. Lippincott. 2013.

calculators.name: MELD-Na Score
calculators.description: Modified MELD incorporating serum sodium. More accurate for liver transplant waitlist mortality.

results.label: MELD-Na Score
results.formula: 3.78 * log(meldNaBili) + 11.2 * log(meldNaInr) + 9.57 * log(meldNaCr) + 6.43 - meldNaNa * (0.025 * meldNaNa + 0.04 * (140 - meldNaNa))

calculators.source: Kim WR et al. Hyponatremia and mortality among patients on the liver-transplant waiting list. N Engl J Med. 2008;359(10):1018-1026.

calculators.name: FIB-4 Index (Liver Fibrosis)
calculators.description: Non-invasive liver fibrosis index using age, AST, ALT, platelets.

interpretations.label: High risk — advanced fibrosis likely (PPV ~80%)
interpretations.detail: FIB-4 >2.67. Refer for liver stiffness assessment (elastography). Hepatology evaluation.

calculators.source: Sterling RK et al. Development of a simple noninvasive index to predict significant fibrosis in patients with HIV/HCV coinfection. Hepatology. 2006;43(6):1317-1325.

calculators.name: APRI Score (Liver Fibrosis)
calculators.description: AST-to-Platelet Ratio Index. Validated non-invasive marker for hepatic fibrosis in hepatitis C and NAFLD.

interpretations.label: High risk — cirrhosis likely
interpretations.detail: APRI >1.5. Refer for liver stiffness measurement and hepatology evaluation.

calculators.source: Wai CT et al. A simple noninvasive index can predict both significant fibrosis and cirrhosis in patients with chronic hepatitis C. Hepatology. 2003;38(2):518-526.

calculators.name: AIMS65 (GI Bleed Mortality)
calculators.description: Simple 5-point score for predicting in-hospital mortality in upper GI bleeding.

interpretations.label: Mortality ~8% — moderate risk
interpretations.detail: AIMS65 ≥2 suggests higher mortality. ICU admission recommended.

calculators.source: Saltzman JR et al. A simple risk score accurately predicts in-hospital mortality, length of stay, and cost in acute upper GI hemorrhage. Gastrointest Endosc. 2011;74(6):1215-1224.

calculators.name: Oakland Score (Lower GI Bleed)
calculators.description: Predicts safe discharge from ED after lower GI bleeding. Score ≤8 suggests safe discharge with outpatient follow-up.
calculators.transparency_template:
Oakland Score = age + sex + hb + sbp + hr + bleeding + tender + aspirin
= {oaklandScore}

≤8 consider safe discharge
>8 admission required

interpretations.label: Safe for discharge — outpatient follow-up
interpretations.detail: Oakland ≤8. Low risk of adverse outcome. Schedule outpatient colonoscopy within 2-4 weeks.

interpretations.label: Admit for further management
interpretations.detail: Oakland >8. Inpatient management recommended.

calculators.source: Oakland K et al. Derivation and validation of a novel risk score for safe discharge after acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: a modelling study. Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2017;2(9):635-643.

calculators.name: Total Blood Volume (Nadler)
calculators.description: Estimates total blood volume using Nadler formula. Used for transfusion and apheresis planning.
calculators.transparency_template:
Nadler Formula:
{tbvMale ? "Male" : "Female"}: {tbv} L

Plasma volume: {tbv * (1 - 0.45)} L approx
RBC volume: {tbv * 0.45} L approx
calculators.source: Nadler SB et al. Prediction of blood volume in normal human adults. Surgery. 1962;51(2):224-232.

calculators.name: Calvert Formula (Carboplatin AUC)
calculators.description: Calculates carboplatin dose for target AUC using Calvert formula: Dose = target AUC × (GFR + 25).
calculators.transparency_template:
Carboplatin Dose = AUC × (GFR + 25)
= {calvertAuc} × ({calvertGfr} + 25)
= {calvertDose} mg

Round to nearest 50 mg for administration.
Check platelet count nadir.
calculators.source: Calvert AH et al. Carboplatin dosage: prospective evaluation of a simple formula based on renal function. J Clin Oncol. 1989;7(11):1748-1756.

calculators.name: Tumor Lysis Syndrome (Cairo-Bishop)
calculators.description: Cairo-Bishop criteria classify laboratory and clinical TLS risk.

interpretations.label: Lab TLS — ≥2 criteria met
interpretations.detail: Aggressive IV hydration, rasburicase/allopurinol, monitor cardiac rhythm, manage K/PO4/Ca.

interpretations.label: Lab TLS not met — continue prophylaxis
interpretations.detail: <2 lab abnormalities. Continue hydration, monitoring, and prophylaxis as per risk category.

calculators.source: Cairo MS, Bishop M. Tumour lysis syndrome: new therapeutic strategies and classification. Br J Haematol. 2004;127(1):3-11.

calculators.name: QUICKI Index (Insulin Sensitivity)
calculators.description: Quantitative Insulin Sensitivity Check Index. 1 / (log(fasting insulin) + log(fasting glucose)). Inverse of HOMA-IR.
calculators.transparency_template:
QUICKI = 1 / (log(insulin) + log(glucose))
= 1 / (log({qInsulin}) + log({qGlucose}))
= {quickiIdx}

Normal: >0.357 | IR: <0.330
calculators.source: Katz A et al. Quantitative insulin sensitivity check index: a simple, accurate method for assessing insulin sensitivity in humans. J Clin Endocrinol Metab. 2000;85(7):2402-2410.

calculators.name: Calcium-Phosphate Product
calculators.description: CaPO4 product = calcium × phosphorus in mg/dL. Normal <55. High levels increase vascular calcification risk in CKD.
calculators.transparency_template:
CaPO4 = Ca × PO4 = {capo4Ca} × {capo4Phos} = {capo4Product} mg²/dL²

CKD target: <55
>55: increased vascular calcification risk
calculators.source: Block GA, Port FK. Re-evaluation of risks associated with hyperphosphatemia and hyperparathyroidism in dialysis patients: recommendations for a change in management. Am J Kidney Dis. 2000;35(6):1226-1237.

calculators.name: DKA Severity Classification
calculators.description: ADA/JBDS classification of DKA severity based on pH, bicarbonate, and anion gap.

interpretations.label: Mild DKA
interpretations.detail: pH 7.25-7.30, HCO3 15-18. IV fluids, fixed-rate insulin infusion. Monitor K, glucose, ketones hourly.

interpretations.label: Moderate DKA
interpretations.detail: pH 7.00-7.24, HCO3 10-14. Insulin infusion, aggressive fluids. Check for precipitant.

interpretations.label: Severe DKA
interpretations.detail: pH <7.00, HCO3 <10. ICU/HDU admission. High-risk monitoring. Consider central access if shocked.

calculators.source: Kitabchi AE et al. Hyperglycemic crises in adult patients with diabetes: a consensus statement from the ADA. Diabetes Care. 2009;32(7):1335-1343.

calculators.name: HIV CD4/CD8 Ratio
calculators.description: CD4/CD8 ratio and CD4 percentage assessment for HIV monitoring. Normal ratio >1.0. Inverted ratio suggests immune dysfunction.
calculators.transparency_template:
CD4/CD8 Ratio = {cd4Count} / {cd8Count} = {cd4Cd8Ratio}

Normal: >1.0
Inverted (<1.0): HIV progression, immune senescence

OI Risk:
CD4 <500: TB risk increased
CD4 <200: PCP, crypto, toxo risk
CD4 <100: MAC, CMV risk
calculators.source: CDC. Guidelines for the Use of Antiretroviral Agents in HIV-1-Infected Adults and Adolescents. 2023.

calculators.name: Typhoid Widal Test Interpretation
calculators.description: Interpretation of Widal test for typhoid fever in endemic areas. Single titre ≥1:160 or 4-fold rise is significant.
calculators.transparency_template:
Widal Test:
O titre: {widalO} | H titre: {widalH}

In endemic areas:
Single O ≥1:160 or H ≥1:160: suggestive
4-fold rise between acute/convalescent: diagnostic

False positives: malaria, dengue, immunisation, chronic liver disease

interpretations.label: Positive — suggestive of typhoid
interpretations.detail: O or H ≥1:160. In endemic areas, supports clinical diagnosis. Start empiric antibiotics while awaiting culture.

interpretations.label: Negative — typhoid less likely
interpretations.detail: Low titres. Consider other causes of febrile illness (malaria, dengue, Lassa, UTI). Cultures if suspicion remains.

calculators.source: WHO. The diagnosis, treatment and prevention of typhoid fever. 2003.

calculators.name: Paediatric BSA (Haycock)
calculators.description: Haycock formula for paediatric BSA. Most accurate for children: BSA = 0.024265 × W^0.5378 × H^0.3964.
calculators.transparency_template:
Haycock BSA = 0.024265 × W^0.5378 × H^0.3964
= {paedsBsa} m²

Preferred for children. Used for chemotherapy dosing.
calculators.source: Haycock GB et al. Geometric method for measuring body surface area: a height-weight formula validated in infants, children, and adults. J Pediatr. 1978;93(1):62-66.

calculators.name: Paediatric GCS (Modified)
calculators.description: Modified Glasgow Coma Scale for pre-verbal children/infants. Eye 1-4, Verbal 1-5, Motor 1-6.

options.label: 4 - Cries but consolable, less than usual words

calculators.transparency_template:
Paediatric GCS:
Eyes: {pgcsEye}/4 | Verbal: {pgcsVerbal}/5 | Motor: {pgcsMotor}/6
Total: {pgcsTotal}/15

interpretations.label: Moderate brain injury (GCS 9-12)
interpretations.detail: GCS 9-12. CT head indicated. Observe for deterioration.

interpretations.label: Severe brain injury (GCS ≤8) — intubation likely
interpretations.detail: GCS ≤8. Severe traumatic brain injury. Intubate and ventilate. Urgent CT head. PICU admission.

calculators.source: Reilly PL et al. GCS for children. Lancet. 1981;2(8243):439.

calculators.name: Palivizumab Dosing (RSV)
calculators.description: Palivizumab (Synagis) 15 mg/kg IM monthly for RSV prophylaxis in high-risk infants.
calculators.transparency_template:
Palivizumab = 15 mg/kg × {palWt} kg = {palDose} mg
Volume (100 mg/mL) = {palVolume} mL

Give IM monthly during RSV season (5 doses).
Max dose: 300 mg.
calculators.source: American Academy of Pediatrics. Updated guidance for palivizumab prophylaxis. Pediatrics. 2014;134(2):415-420.

calculators.name: Young's Rule (Paediatric Dose)
calculators.description: Historical paediatric dose formula: Child dose = (Age / (Age + 12)) × Adult dose. For ages 1-12 years.
calculators.transparency_template:
Young's Rule: Dose = (Age / (Age + 12)) × Adult Dose
= ({youngAge}/({youngAge}+12)) × {youngAdult}
= {youngDose} mg

For ages 1-12. NOT weight-based. Use with caution.
calculators.source: Lack JA, Stuart-Taylor M. Drug calculations in children. Br J Anaesth. 1997;78(4):481.

calculators.name: Fried's Rule (Infant Dose)
calculators.description: Dose = (Age in months / 150) × Adult dose. For infants <1 year (up to 2 years).
calculators.transparency_template:
Fried's Rule: Dose = (Months / 150) × Adult Dose
= ({friedMos}/150) × {friedAdult}
= {friedDose} mg

For infants <2 years. Approximate only.
calculators.source: Fried AI. Pediatric dosing: a review of problems and approaches. Drug Intell Clin Pharm. 1980;14(5):352-358.

calculators.name: Clark's Rule (Paediatric Dose by Weight)
calculators.description: Clark's Rule: Child dose = (Weight in kg / 68.2) × Adult dose.
calculators.transparency_template:
Clark's Rule: Dose = (Child Wt / 68.2) × Adult Dose
= ({clarkWt}/68.2) × {clarkAdult}
= {clarkDose} mg

Based on standard adult weight of 68.2 kg (150 lbs).
calculators.source: Lack JA, Stuart-Taylor M. Drug calculations in children. Br J Anaesth. 1997;78(4):481.

calculators.name: Neonatal Jaundice (Bhutani Nomogram)
calculators.description: Interpretation of plotted TSB on Bhutani nomogram for phototherapy and exchange transfusion thresholds by gestational age and risk factors.
calculators.transparency_template:
Bhutani Nomogram (AAP 2022):
TSB: {bhuTsb} mg/dL | Age: {bhuHours}h | GA: {bhuGestation == 0 ? "≥38wk no RF" : (bhuGestation == 1 ? "≥38wk + RF" : "35-37wk")}

Phototherapy threshold: >{bhuPhototherapy} mg/dL
Exchange threshold: >{bhuExchange} mg/dL

interpretations.label: Below phototherapy threshold — monitor
interpretations.detail: TSB {bhuTsb}. Recheck in 8-12h if early, or 24h if near discharge.

interpretations.label: Phototherapy indicated
interpretations.detail: Start intensive phototherapy. Recheck TSB in 4-6h. Assess for haemolysis (G6PD, blood type).

interpretations.label: Exchange transfusion threshold
interpretations.detail: TSB at exchange transfusion level. Prepare for exchange if phototherapy fails to lower TSB by 2 mg/dL in 4-6h.

calculators.source: Bhutani VK et al. Predictive ability of a predischarge hour-specific serum bilirubin for subsequent significant hyperbilirubinemia in healthy term and near-term newborns. Pediatrics. 1999;103(1):6-14.

calculators.name: Neonatal Hypoglycaemia Thresholds
calculators.description: AAP/PES operational thresholds for neonatal hypoglycaemia management by postnatal age and risk factors.
calculators.transparency_template:
Neonatal Hypoglycaemia (AAP/PES):
Glucose: {neoHypoGlucose} mg/dL

Operational thresholds:
0-4h: <25 mg/dL treat
4-24h: <35 mg/dL treat (or <45 if symptomatic)
>24h: <45 mg/dL treat

Any symptomatic + <50 mg/dL: treat immediately

interpretations.label: Hypoglycaemia — treat
interpretations.detail: Glucose below operational threshold. Breastfeed/feed, consider IV D10W 2 mL/kg. Recheck in 30 min.

interpretations.label: Symptomatic hypoglycaemia — emergency
interpretations.detail: Symptomatic + glucose <50. IV D10W 2 mL/kg stat. Recheck glucose. Admit to NICU. Consider IV dextrose infusion.

calculators.source: Adamkin DH et al. Postnatal glucose homeostasis in late-preterm and term infants. Pediatrics. 2011;127(3):575-579.

calculators.name: Gestational Age (LMP)
calculators.description: Calculates gestational age in weeks + days from LMP or known EDD.
calculators.transparency_template:
GA = Days since LMP ÷ 7
= {gaDays} days / 7
= {gaWeeks} weeks

Note: Simple estimate. Confirm with ultrasound in 1st trimester.
calculators.source: ACOG Committee on Obstetric Practice. Methods for estimating the due date. Committee Opinion No. 700. Obstet Gynecol. 2017;129(5):e150-e154.

calculators.name: Biophysical Profile (BPP) Score
calculators.description: Manning BPP: 5 components each scored 0-2. Total 0-10. Used for fetal well-being assessment.
