interpretations.label: Equivocal — repeat in 12-24h
interpretations.detail: BPP 6. Assess for oligohydramnios. Consider delivery if term or further evaluation.

interpretations.label: Abnormal — urgent delivery likely
interpretations.detail: BPP ≤4. Fetal acidosis possible. Urgent delivery indicated unless extreme prematurity.

calculators.source: Manning FA et al. Fetal biophysical profile scoring: a prospective study in 1,184 high-risk pregnancies. Am J Obstet Gynecol. 1981;140(3):289-294.

calculators.name: Magnesium Sulfate Regimens (Eclampsia)
calculators.description: Pritchard (IM/IV) and Zuspan (IV-only) regimens for eclampsia prophylaxis/treatment per ACOG.
calculators.transparency_template:
Magnesium Sulfate Regimens:

Pritchard (IM/IV):
Loading: 4 g IV + 5 g IM (gluteal) = {pritchardLoading} g
Maintenance: 5 g IM q4h

Zuspan (IV):
Bolus: 4 g IV over 15-20 min
Infusion: 1 g/h IV × 24h

Mg target: 4.8-8.4 mg/dL
Monitor: RR, DTRs, urine output q1h
calculators.source: Collaborative Eclampsia Trial. Which anticonvulsant for women with eclampsia? Lancet. 1995;345(8963):1455-1463. (Magnesium sulphate vs phenytoin/diazepam)

calculators.name: PCOS Diagnosis (Rotterdam Criteria)
calculators.description: Rotterdam criteria: ≥2 of 3 (oligo/anovulation, hyperandrogenism, PCOM on US) needed for PCOS diagnosis.

inputs.label: PCOM on ultrasound (≥12 follicles or ovarian volume >10 mL)

interpretations.label: PCOS diagnosed (≥2 of 3 criteria)
interpretations.detail: PCOS by Rotterdam criteria. Assess metabolic status (OGTT, lipids). Manage according to phenotype and goals (fertility, metabolic, cosmetic).

interpretations.label: PCOS cannot be diagnosed — <2 criteria
interpretations.detail: Does not meet Rotterdam criteria. Consider other causes of oligo/anovulation or hyperandrogenism.

calculators.source: Rotterdam ESHRE/ASRM-Sponsored PCOS Consensus Workshop Group. Revised 2003 consensus on diagnostic criteria and long-term health risks related to polycystic ovary syndrome. Fertil Steril. 2004;81(1):19-25.

calculators.name: Gestational Diabetes OGTT (IADPSG/WHO)
calculators.description: IADPSG/WHO 2013 criteria for 75g OGTT at 24-28 weeks. Any single threshold met = GDM diagnosis.
calculators.transparency_template:
IADPSG/WHO 75g OGTT (24-28 weeks):

Thresholds:
Fasting ≥92 mg/dL
1 hour ≥180 mg/dL
2 hour ≥153 mg/dL

Fasting: {gdmFasting} | 1h: {gdm1h} | 2h: {gdm2h}
Criteria exceeded: {gdmResult}/3

interpretations.label: GDM diagnosed — ≥1 threshold met
interpretations.detail: Any single threshold exceeded confirms GDM. Start dietary counselling, glucose monitoring. Consider metformin/insulin if targets not met.

calculators.source: IADPSG Consensus Panel. Recommendations on the diagnosis and classification of hyperglycemia in pregnancy. Diabetes Care. 2010;33(3):676-682.

calculators.name: STOP-BANG (OSA Screening)
calculators.description: 8-item screening for obstructive sleep apnea. Score 0-8. ≥3 suggests OSA risk.

inputs.label: Snoring (loud enough to hear through door)

results.label: STOP-BANG Score
results.formula: snore + tired + observed + bpStop + bmiStop + ageStop + neckStop + genderStop

interpretations.label: Intermediate risk — refer for sleep study
interpretations.detail: STOP-BANG 3-4. Moderate risk of OSA. Consider polysomnography.

interpretations.label: High risk — high probability of moderate-severe OSA
interpretations.detail: STOP-BANG ≥5. High probability of moderate-to-severe OSA. Urgent sleep referral. CPAP likely.

calculators.source: Chung F et al. STOP-Bang Questionnaire: a practical approach to screen for obstructive sleep apnea. Chest. 2016;149(3):631-638.

calculators.name: Caprini Risk Score (VTE Prophylaxis)
calculators.description: Caprini risk assessment model for VTE in surgical patients. Each risk factor scored, total estimates VTE risk.

results.label: Caprini Score
results.formula: capAge + capBmi + capSurgery + capLaparoscopic + capMalignancy + capHormone + capPregnancy + capPriorVte + capMobility

interpretations.label: Low risk — early ambulation
interpretations.detail: Caprini ≤1. Early mobilisation only.

interpretations.label: Moderate risk — consider pharmacological prophylaxis
interpretations.detail: Caprini 2-3. Consider LMWH/UFH + mechanical prophylaxis.

interpretations.label: High risk — pharmacological + mechanical
interpretations.detail: Caprini 4-5. LMWH/UFH + compression stockings/IPC. Extended prophylaxis post-op.

interpretations.label: Very high risk — aggressive multimodal prophylaxis
interpretations.detail: Caprini ≥6. LMWH + mechanical. Consider extended prophylaxis for up to 35 days. Monitor for bleeding.

calculators.source: Caprini JA. Risk assessment as a guide for the prevention of the many faces of venous thromboembolism. Am J Surg. 2010;199(1 Suppl):S3-S10.

calculators.name: RCRI (Revised Cardiac Risk Index / Lee Index)
calculators.description: Predicts major cardiac events after non-cardiac surgery. 6 risk factors, each 1 point.

results.label: RCRI (Lee Index)
results.formula: rcriCad + rcriIhd + rcriChf + rcriCva + rcriDiabetes + rcriCr

interpretations.label: MACE risk ~6.6%
interpretations.detail: RCRI 2. Consider preoperative cardiac assessment. Optimise medical therapy.

interpretations.label: MACE risk ≥11%
interpretations.detail: RCRI ≥3. High risk. Cardiology consult. Consider postponing for optimisation.

calculators.source: Lee TH et al. Derivation and prospective validation of a simple index for prediction of cardiac risk of major noncardiac surgery. Circulation. 1999;100(10):1043-1049.

calculators.name: Padua Prediction Score (VTE Prophylaxis)
calculators.description: Risk assessment for VTE in hospitalised medical patients. Score ≥4 indicates high risk.

results.label: Padua Score
results.formula: padActCa + padPriorVte + padMobility + padThrombophilia + padTrauma + padAge + padChf + padMi + padInfection + padObesity

interpretations.label: High VTE risk — pharmacological prophylaxis indicated
interpretations.detail: Padua ≥4. Start LMWH or UFH. Reassess bleeding risk. Contraindications?

interpretations.label: Low VTE risk — no pharmacological prophylaxis
interpretations.detail: Padua <4. Early mobilisation. Reassess if clinical status changes.

calculators.source: Barbar S et al. A risk assessment model for the identification of hospitalized medical patients at risk for venous thromboembolism: the Padua Prediction Score. J Thromb Haemost. 2010;8(11):2450-2457.

calculators.name: ATRIA Bleeding Score
calculators.description: Anticoagulation and Risk Factors in Atrial Fibrillation bleeding risk score. 0-10 points.
calculators.source: Fang MC et al. A new risk scheme to predict warfarin-associated hemorrhage: the ATRIA (Anticoagulation and Risk Factors in Atrial Fibrillation) Study. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2011;58(4):395-401.

calculators.name: GRACE Score (ACS Mortality)
calculators.description: GRACE risk score predicts in-hospital and 6-month mortality after ACS. 8 variables.

results.label: GRACE Score
results.formula: graceAge + graceHr + graceSbp + graceCr + graceKillip + graceSt + graceArrest + graceEnzyme + 0

calculators.source: Fox KAA et al. Prediction of risk of death and myocardial infarction in the six months after presentation with ACS: a prospective, multinational, observational study (GRACE). BMJ. 2006;333(7578):1091.

calculators.name: Fetal Weight Estimation (Hadlock)
calculators.description: Hadlock formula using BPD, HC, AC, FL to estimate fetal weight.

interpretations.label: Macrosomia (>4000g)
interpretations.detail: EFW >4000g. Increased risk of shoulder dystocia, birth trauma. Discuss delivery options.

calculators.source: Hadlock FP et al. Estimation of fetal weight with the use of head, body, and femur measurements. Radiology. 1985;150(2):535-540.

calculators.name: OHSS Risk (Ovarian Hyperstimulation)
calculators.description: Risk classification and management guidance for ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome after fertility treatment.

interpretations.label: Mild OHSS — outpatient management
interpretations.detail: Analgesia, antiemetics, oral fluids. Monitor daily weights, UOP. Avoid intercourse/pregnancy test.

interpretations.label: Moderate OHSS — consider admission
interpretations.detail: Admit if significant pain/nausea. IV fluids, monitor I/O, daily U/S, LFTs, Cr, Hct.

interpretations.label: Severe OHSS — admit urgently
interpretations.detail: IV fluids, albumin, paracentesis if tense ascites. LMWH for VTE prophylaxis. Monitor for renal failure, ARDS.

calculators.source: Golan A, Weissman A. Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome. Fertil Steril. 2009;92(4):1270-1277.

calculators.name: Canadian C-Spine Rule
calculators.description: Validated rule for safe clearance of cervical spine in alert, stable trauma patients.

inputs.label: Dangerous mechanism (fall >1m or 5 stairs, axial load, high-speed, rollover, ejection, bicycle collision)
Able to rotate neck 45° left and right

interpretations.label: Low risk — may clinically clear C-spine
interpretations.detail: No risk factors. Alert, stable patient without dangerous mechanism. Can clear without imaging.

interpretations.label: C-spine imaging indicated (CT)
interpretations.detail: Positive risk factor for CSI. CT C-spine indicated.

calculators.source: Stiell IG et al. The Canadian C-spine rule for radiography in alert and stable trauma patients. JAMA. 2001;286(15):1841-1848.

calculators.name: NEXUS Criteria (C-Spine Clearance)
calculators.description: NEXUS low-risk criteria for cervical spine injury. All must be absent to clinically clear.

interpretations.label: NEXUS negative — low risk, may clinically clear
interpretations.detail: All 5 NEXUS criteria absent. C-spine can be cleared clinically without imaging.

interpretations.label: NEXUS positive — C-spine imaging indicated
interpretations.detail: ≥1 NEXUS criteria present. CT C-spine indicated.

calculators.source: Hoffman JR et al. Validity of a set of clinical criteria to rule out injury to the cervical spine in patients with blunt trauma. N Engl J Med. 2000;343(2):94-99.

calculators.name: Ottawa Ankle & Knee Rules
calculators.description: Validated rules to determine need for ankle/knee X-rays after acute injury.

inputs.label: Tenderness at tip of posterior/inferior malleolus
Unable to bear weight immediately and in ED (4 steps)

interpretations.label: Low risk — no X-ray required
interpretations.detail: Ottawa rules negative. Clinically clear ankle fracture.

interpretations.label: X-ray ankle indicated
interpretations.detail: Ottawa rules positive. Obtain ankle X-ray series.

calculators.source: Stiell IG et al. A study to develop clinical decision rules for the use of radiography in acute ankle injuries. Ann Emerg Med. 1992;21(4):384-390.

calculators.name: Local Anaesthetic Toxicity (Max Safe Dose)
calculators.description: Calculates maximum safe dose of common local anaesthetics (with and without epinephrine).
calculators.transparency_template:
Max LA Dose = Weight × Dose/kg
= {laWt} × {laAgent} = {laMaxDoseMg} mg

Signs of LAST: circumoral numbness, tinnitus, metallic taste, seizures, arrhythmia
Treatment: Intralipid 20% 1.5 mL/kg bolus, then 0.25 mL/kg/min
calculators.source: Neal JM et al. The Third American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine Practice Advisory on Local Anesthetic Systemic Toxicity. Reg Anesth Pain Med. 2018;43(2):113-123.

calculators.name: LAST Protocol (Intralipid Dosing)
calculators.description: ASRA-recommended Intralipid 20% dosing for Local Anaesthetic Systemic Toxicity (LAST) resuscitation. Weight-based.
calculators.transparency_template:
Intralipid 20% for LAST (ASRA protocol):

Bolus: 1.5 mL/kg IV over 1 min = {lipidBolus} mL
May repeat bolus ×2 for persistent CV collapse

Infusion: 0.25 mL/kg/min = {lipidInfusion} mL/min = {lipidInfusionHr} mL/h
Continue for at least 15 min after haemodynamic stability

Max total: 12 mL/kg
calculators.source: Neal JM et al. ASRA practice advisory on local anesthetic systemic toxicity. Reg Anesth Pain Med. 2012;37(1):16-18.

calculators.name: HHS Diagnostic Criteria (Hyperosmolar State)
calculators.description: ADA/JBDS diagnostic criteria for hyperosmolar hyperglycaemic state (HHS).

interpretations.label: HHS confirmed — ≥4 criteria met
interpretations.detail: HHS diagnosed. Glucose >600, Osm >320, pH >7.3, HCO3 >15, absent ketones. ICU admission. Aggressive IV fluids (0.9% NS), insulin 0.05 U/kg/h if glucose not falling with fluids alone.

interpretations.label: HHS not confirmed — consider DKA or mixed picture

calculators.source: ADA. Hyperglycemic crises in diabetes. Diabetes Care. 2004;27(Suppl 1):S94-S102.

calculators.name: Fetal Lung Maturity (TSP:SB ratio)
calculators.description: Lamellar body count / surfactant-to-albumin ratio assessment for fetal lung maturity via amniocentesis.
calculators.transparency_template:
Fetal Lung Maturity (L/S ratio):
Ratio: {tspRatio}

≥2.0: Mature — low risk of RDS
<2.0: Immature — increased RDS risk

interpretations.label: Immature — high RDS risk
interpretations.detail: Consider betamethasone 24h before delivery if <34 weeks.

calculators.source: Gluck L et al. Diagnosis of the respiratory distress syndrome by amniocentesis. Am J Obstet Gynecol. 1971;109(3):440-445.

calculators.name: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS)
calculators.description: 10-item screening for perinatal depression. Each item 0-3. Score ≥10 or any self-harm response triggers referral.

inputs.label: Able to laugh/see funny side (reverse scored)
Blamed myself unnecessarily when things went wrong
Things getting on top of me
So unhappy that I have had difficulty sleeping
Unhappy so I have been crying

results.label: EPDS Score
results.formula: epds1 + epds2 + epds3 + epds4 + epds5 + epds6 + epds7 + epds8 + epds9 + epds10

calculators.transparency_template: EPDS Score: {epdsTotal}/30

interpretations.label: Borderline — possible depression
interpretations.detail: EPDS 10-12. Assess further. May need follow-up in 2 weeks. Offer counselling.

interpretations.label: Positive screen — probable depression
interpretations.detail: EPDS ≥13. Likely depression. Refer to perinatal mental health. Assess risk of self-harm.

calculators.source: Cox JL, Holden JM, Sagovsky R. Detection of postnatal depression. Development of the 10-item EPDS. Br J Psychiatry. 1987;150:782-786.

calculators.name: Cervical Cancer Risk (ASCCP Guidelines)
calculators.description: Simplified cervical cancer screening pathway based on Pap smear and HPV status per ASCCP risk-based guidelines.

interpretations.label: Repeat co-testing in 1 year recommended
interpretations.detail: ASC-US + HPV-positive (non-16/18). Repeat in 1 year per ASCCP.

interpretations.label: Colposcopy referral indicated
interpretations.detail: HSIL/ASC-H or HPV 16/18 or co-testing high-grade. Refer for colposcopy + biopsy.

calculators.source: Perkins RB et al. 2019 ASCCP Risk-Based Management Consensus Guidelines for Abnormal Cervical Cancer Screening Tests and Cancer Precursors. J Low Genit Tract Dis. 2020;24(2):102-131.

calculators.name: Dengue Severity (WHO 2009 Classification)
calculators.description: WHO 2009 dengue classification: Dengue without warning signs, Dengue with warning signs, or Severe Dengue.

inputs.label: Fever + 2 of: nausea/vomiting, rash, aches, positive tourniquet
Rising Hct with rapid platelet drop
Severe organ involvement (AST/ALT ≥1000, impaired consciousness, myocarditis)

results.label: Warning signs present
results.formula: dengueAbdPain + dengueVomiting + dengueLeak + dengueBleed + dengueLethargy + dengueLiver + dengueHct

interpretations.label: Severe Dengue — ICU admission
interpretations.detail: Shock, severe bleeding, or severe organ involvement. ICU care, fluid resuscitation, transfusion as needed.

interpretations.label: Dengue with warning signs — hospital admission
interpretations.detail: Admit for close monitoring. IV isotonic fluids, platelet monitoring, vital signs 4-hourly.

interpretations.label: Dengue without warning signs — outpatient management
interpretations.detail: Paracetamol (avoid NSAIDs/aspirin). Oral fluids. Return if warning signs develop.

calculators.source: WHO. Dengue: Guidelines for Diagnosis, Treatment, Prevention and Control. 2009 edition.

calculators.name: Measles Complication Risk Assessment
calculators.description: WHO-based assessment of measles severity and complication risk. Identifies patients needing hospital referral.

results.label: Complication risk
results.formula: measlesAge + measlesMalnutrition + measlesImmuno + measlesPneumonia + measlesDiarrhoea + measlesOtitis + measlesNeuro + measlesEye

interpretations.label: Uncomplicated measles — home care
interpretations.detail: Vitamin A, paracetamol, nutrition, eye care. Return if complications develop.

interpretations.label: Risk factors present — close follow-up
interpretations.detail: Treat complications. Consider hospital referral if <12mo or malnourished.

calculators.source: WHO. Measles vaccines: WHO position paper. WER. 2017;92(17):205-228.

calculators.name: Bacterial Meningitis Score (BMS)
calculators.description: Identifies patients at very low risk of bacterial meningitis who may avoid LP and empiric antibiotics. For children 2 months–18 years.

interpretations.label: Very low risk — consider deferring LP/antibiotics
interpretations.detail: BMS negative. <0.5% risk of bacterial meningitis. Observe closely, treat alternative if suspected viral.

interpretations.label: Risk of bacterial meningitis — LP and empiric antibiotics indicated
interpretations.detail: BMS positive. Start empiric antibiotics (ceftriaxone + vancomycin) and perform LP if safe.

calculators.source: Nigrovic LE et al. Clinical prediction rule for identifying children with cerebrospinal fluid pleocytosis at very low risk of bacterial meningitis. JAMA. 2007;297(1):52-60.

calculators.name: Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HAM-D 17)
calculators.description: 17-item clinician-rated scale for depression severity. Score 0–52. Most widely used depression rating scale.

options.label: 1 — Feels life is not worth living
1 — Wakes early but can fall back
1 — Thoughts of incompetence, fatigue with activities
3 — Reduced actual time in activities or productivity
1 — Loss of appetite, eating without encouragement
2 — Difficulty eating without urging, laxatives needed
1 — Heaviness in limbs, back, or head; diffuse aches

results.label: HAM-D Total
results.formula: hamdDepressed + hamdGuilt + hamdSuicide + hamdInsomniaEarly + hamdInsomniaMiddle + hamdInsomniaLate + hamdWork + hamdRetardation + hamdAgitation + hamdAnxietyPsych + hamdAnxietySom + hamdGi + hamdGeneral + hamdLibido + hamdHypochondria + hamdWeight + hamdInsight

interpretations.label: Very severe depression
interpretations.detail: HAM-D ≥23. Urgent psychiatric referral. Assess suicide risk.

calculators.source: Hamilton M. A rating scale for depression. J Neurol Neurosurg Psychiatry. 1960;23:56-62.

calculators.name: Young Mania Rating Scale (YMRS)
calculators.description: 11-item clinician-rated scale for mania severity. Score 0–60. Used for diagnosis and treatment monitoring in bipolar disorder.

options.label: 2 — Admits behaviour change but denies illness

results.label: YMRS Total
results.formula: ymrsElevated + ymrsEnergy + ymrsSexual + ymrsSleep + ymrsIrritable + ymrsSpeech + ymrsLanguage + ymrsContent + ymrsDisruptive + ymrsAppearance + ymrsInsight

interpretations.label: Severe mania — urgent psychiatric care
interpretations.detail: YMRS ≥30. Consider hospitalisation. Assess safety, start mood stabiliser ± antipsychotic.

calculators.source: Young RC et al. A rating scale for mania: reliability, validity and sensitivity. Br J Psychiatry. 1978;133:429-435.

calculators.name: C-SSRS Screening (Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale)
calculators.description: Suicide risk screening. Lifetime/recent ideation and behaviour. Any positive response warrants further assessment.

inputs.label: 2. Non-specific suicidal thoughts ("I've thought about killing myself")
3. Suicidal ideation with method (any thought of how to do it)
4. Suicidal intent ("I have some intent to carry it out")
5. Suicidal intent with plan (specific plan, time, place)
6. Any suicidal behaviour (preparatory acts, aborted attempt, interrupted attempt, actual attempt)

results.label: C-SSRS Severity Index
results.formula: cssrsWish + cssrsThoughts + cssrsMethod + cssrsIntent + cssrsPlan + cssrsBehaviour

results.label: Highest ideation level
results.formula: max(cssrsWish, max(cssrsThoughts, max(cssrsMethod, max(cssrsIntent, max(cssrsPlan, cssrsBehaviour)))))

interpretations.label: No current suicidal ideation or behaviour
Passive ideation — monitor closely
interpretations.detail: Wish to be dead or non-specific thoughts without plan/intent. Provide crisis resources. Follow up within 24h.

interpretations.label: Active ideation — urgent mental health referral
interpretations.detail: Method, intent, or preparatory behaviour present. Do not leave patient alone. Urgent psychiatric evaluation.

interpretations.label: High risk — immediate safety intervention
interpretations.detail: Suicidal intent with plan or recent behaviour. One-to-one observation. Emergency psychiatric assessment. Consider compulsory admission if needed.

calculators.source: Posner K et al. The Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale: initial validity and internal consistency findings from three multisite studies with adolescents and adults. Am J Psychiatry. 2011;168(12):1266-1277.

calculators.name: Modified Early Warning Score (MEWS)
calculators.description: Simple physiologic score for ward patients to identify early deterioration. Score 0–14. Triggers escalation of care.
calculators.transparency_template:
MEWS:
HR: {mewsHr} | SBP: {mewsSbp} | RR: {mewsRr} | Temp: {mewsTemp} | AVPU: {mewsAvpu}
Total: {mewsTotal}/14

0-2: Continue routine monitoring
3-4: Increase obs frequency, inform nurse-in-charge
≥5: Urgent medical review, consider ICU outreach
≥7: Critical — immediate team response

interpretations.label: Moderate risk — increase observation frequency
interpretations.detail: Inform nurse-in-charge. Vital signs 2-hourly. Senior nurse review within 30 min.

interpretations.label: High risk — urgent medical review
interpretations.detail: MEWS ≥5. Doctor review within 15 min. Consider ICU outreach, call MET/rapid response.

interpretations.label: Critical — immediate team response
interpretations.detail: MEWS ≥7. Immediate medical emergency team activation. Resuscitation equipment at bedside.

calculators.source: Stenhouse C et al. Prospective evaluation of a modified Early Warning Score to aid earlier detection of patients developing critical illness on an acute surgical ward. Br J Anaesth. 1999;82(5):776-781.

calculators.name: National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS2)
calculators.description: Royal College of Physicians early warning score for acute illness severity. Score 0–20 with optional SpO₂ scale for hypercapnic patients.

results.label: NEWS2 Score
results.formula: news2Rr + news2SpO2 + news2Oxygen + news2Sbp + news2Hr + news2Conscious + news2Temp

calculators.transparency_template:
NEWS2:
RR: {news2Rr} | SpO₂: {news2SpO2} | O₂: {news2Oxygen} | SBP: {news2Sbp}
HR: {news2Hr} | Conscious: {news2Conscious} | Temp: {news2Temp}
Total: {news2Total}/20

0-4: Low — ward obs 4-12 hourly
5-6: Low-medium — increase obs
7+: High — urgent review (MET call if ≥7)
Single parameter ≥3: Escalate

interpretations.label: Low-medium risk — increase monitoring frequency
interpretations.detail: NEWS2 5-6. Nurse-in-charge notified. Vital signs 1-2 hourly. Senior nurse review.

interpretations.label: High risk — urgent medical review
interpretations.detail: NEWS2 ≥7. MET/critical care outreach team review. Consider transfer to HDU/ICU.

calculators.source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2. 2017.

calculators.name: SAPS 3 (Simplified Acute Physiology Score III)
calculators.description: ICU mortality prediction score. 20 physiologic and admission variables. Higher score = higher predicted mortality.

results.label: SAPS 3 Score
results.formula: sapsAge + sapsHr + sapsSbp + sapsTemp + sapsRr + sapsVent + sapsGcs + sapsCreatinine + sapsBilirubin + sapsPlatelets + sapsSodium + sapsGlucose + sapsHco3

interpretations.label: Critical severity — very high mortality
interpretations.detail: SAPS 3 ≥71. High predicted ICU mortality. Discuss goals of care with family.

calculators.source: Metnitz PG et al. SAPS 3—From evaluation of the patient to evaluation of the intensive care unit. Part 1: Objectives, methods and cohort description. Intensive Care Med. 2005;31(10):1336-1344.

calculators.name: MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference)
calculators.description: MUAC is a rapid screening tool for acute malnutrition in children 6–59 months and adults. Uses WHO cutoffs.
calculators.transparency_template:
MUAC: {muacCm} cm ({muacAdult == 0 ? "Child 6-59mo" : "Adult"})

Child cutoffs:
  ≥12.5: Normal
  11.5-12.4: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM)
  <11.5: Severe acute malnutrition (SAM)

Adult cutoffs:
  ≥23.0: Normal
  17.0-22.9: Moderate malnutrition
  <17.0: Severe malnutrition

interpretations.label: Child: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM)
interpretations.detail: MUAC 11.5-12.4cm. Refer to supplementary feeding programme. RUTF supplementation. Follow up monthly.

interpretations.label: Child: Severe acute malnutrition (SAM)
interpretations.detail: MUAC <11.5cm. Urgent referral to therapeutic feeding centre. Treat for complications (hypoglycaemia, hypothermia, infection). F-75 then F-100.

calculators.source: WHO/UNICEF. WHO child growth standards and the identification of severe acute malnutrition in infants and children. 2009.

calculators.name: Westley Croup Severity Score
calculators.description: Assesses croup severity to guide management. Score 0-17. Uses stridor, retractions, air entry, cyanosis, and consciousness.
calculators.transparency_template:
Westley Croup Score:
Stridor: {croupStridor} | Retractions: {croupRetractions} | Air entry: {croupAirEntry}
Cyanosis: {croupCyanosis} | Consciousness: {croupConscious}
Total: {croupTotal}/17

≤2: Mild — outpatient, single dose dexamethasone 0.15-0.6 mg/kg
3-7: Moderate — admit, dexamethasone, racemic epinephrine if needed
8-11: Severe — ICU, close monitoring
≥12: Impending respiratory failure — ICU, prepare for intubation

interpretations.label: Mild croup — outpatient management
interpretations.detail: Dexamethasone 0.15-0.6 mg/kg PO single dose. Discharge with croup counselling.

interpretations.label: Moderate croup — hospital admission
interpretations.detail: Admit. Dexamethasone 0.15-0.6 mg/kg PO/IV/IM. Consider nebulised epinephrine if moderate-severe distress.

interpretations.label: Severe croup — ICU-level care
interpretations.detail: Admit to ICU. Dexamethasone ± prednisolone. Nebulised epinephrine. Monitor closely for fatigue.

interpretations.label: Impending respiratory failure — prepare for intubation
interpretations.detail: Westley ≥12. ICU admission. Intubation cart at bedside. Consider Heliox. ENT referral for rigid bronchoscopy if atypical.

calculators.source: Westley CR et al. Nebulized racemic epinephrine by IPPB for the treatment of croup. Am J Dis Child. 1978;132(5):484-487.

calculators.name: Pediatric Trauma Score (PTS)
calculators.description: Triage tool for injured children. Score -6 to +12. Predicts mortality and need for trauma centre referral.

results.label: Pediatric Trauma Score
results.formula: ptsSize + ptsAirway + ptsSbp + ptsCns + ptsWound + ptsFracture

calculators.transparency_template:
PTS:
Size: {ptsSize} | Airway: {ptsAirway} | SBP: {ptsSbp}
CNS: {ptsCns} | Wound: {ptsWound} | Fracture: {ptsFracture}
Total: {ptsTotal} (-6 to +12)

≥8: Good prognosis, consider non-trauma centre
≤7: Significant injury risk, transfer to trauma centre
≤4: High mortality risk
≤0: Critical — very high mortality

interpretations.label: Low risk — good prognosis
interpretations.detail: PTS ≥8. Survival >95%. May be managed at non-trauma centre.

interpretations.label: Moderate risk — consider trauma centre referral
interpretations.detail: PTS 5-7. Significant injury possible. Refer to trauma centre if available.

interpretations.label: High risk — transfer to trauma centre
interpretations.detail: PTS 1-4. High mortality. Urgent transfer to paediatric trauma centre.

interpretations.label: Critical — very high mortality
interpretations.detail: PTS ≤0. Mortality >50%. Activate trauma team, prepare for massive transfusion.

calculators.source: Tepas JJ 3rd et al. The Pediatric Trauma Score as a predictor of injury severity. J Pediatr Surg. 1987;22(1):14-18.

calculators.name: RIFLE/AKIN AKI Criteria
calculators.description: Standardised classification for acute kidney injury. Compares current Cr and UOP to baseline. Three severity stages.
calculators.transparency_template:
AKI Assessment (KDIGO):
Current Cr: {akiCrCurrent} | Baseline: {akiCrBaseline}
Cr ratio: {akiCrRatio} | Increase: {akiCrIncrease} mg/dL
UOP last 6h: {akiUop6h} mL/kg

Stage 1: Cr ×1.5-1.9 or ≥0.3 mg/dL ↑ or UOP <0.5mL/kg ×6h
Stage 2: Cr ×2.0-2.9 or UOP <0.5mL/kg ×12h
Stage 3: Cr ×3.0 or ≥4.0 or UOP <0.3mL/kg ×24h or RRT

AKI Stage: {akiStage}

interpretations.label: Stage 1 AKI — mild, review nephrotoxins
interpretations.detail: Cr ×1.5-1.9 or ≥0.3 mg/dL rise, or UOP <0.5 mL/kg ×6h. Stop nephrotoxins. Correct volume. Monitor Cr and UOP 6-hourly.

interpretations.label: Stage 2 AKI — escalate monitoring
interpretations.detail: Cr ×2.0-2.9 or UOP <0.5 mL/kg ×12h. Nephrology review. Consider renal ultrasound. Avoid contrast.

interpretations.label: Stage 3 AKI — severe, consider RRT
interpretations.detail: Cr ×3.0 or ≥4.0 or UOP <0.3 mL/kg ×24h or RRT initiated. Urgent nephrology consult. Discuss RRT indications (refractory acidosis, K+ >6.5, uraemic complications, fluid overload).

calculators.source: Bellomo R et al. Acute renal failure - definition, outcome measures, animal models, fluid therapy and information technology needs. Crit Care Med. 2004;32(4):1059-1064. (RIFLE) | Mehta RL et al. Acute Kidney Injury Network. Crit Care. 2007;11(2):R31. (AKIN)

calculators.name: MASCC Score (Febrile Neutropenia Risk)
calculators.description: Multinational Association for Supportive Care in Cancer score identifies low-risk febrile neutropenia patients for oral antibiotics or outpatient management.

results.label: MASCC Score
results.formula: masccBurden + masccHypot + masccCopd + masccSolid + masccFungal + masccDehyd + masccOutpatient + masccAge

interpretations.label: Low risk (≥21) — outpatient/ oral antibiotics
interpretations.detail: MASCC ≥21. Low-risk febrile neutropenia. Consider oral antibiotics (ciprofloxacin + amoxicillin/clavulanate). Outpatient management if reliable follow-up.

interpretations.label: High risk (<21) — IV antibiotics, admission
interpretations.detail: MASCC <21. High-risk febrile neutropenia. IV broad-spectrum antibiotics (piperacillin-tazobactam or cefepime). Hospital admission. G-CSF consideration.

calculators.source: Klastersky J et al. The MASCC risk index score for identifying low-risk febrile neutropenic cancer patients. J Clin Oncol. 2000;18(16):3038-3051.

calculators.name: Vancomycin AUC-guided Dosing
calculators.description: Calculates vancomycin dose and interval to achieve target AUC₂₄₀₀₋₆₅₀ mg·h/L (2020 ASHP/IDSA guideline). Inputs: actual trough, or two levels for AUC calculation.
calculators.transparency_template:
Vancomycin AUC-guided dosing (ASHP/IDSA 2020):

Current: {vanActualDose} mg q{vanInterval}h = {vanDailyDose} mg/day ({vanDosePerKg} mg/kg/day)
CrCl: {vanCrCl} mL/min

Target AUC₂₄: 400-650 mg·h/L
Loading: 25-30 mg/kg if critically ill
Maintenance: 15-20 mg/kg q{vanRecInterval}h, adjusted by CrCl

CrCl-adjusted maintenance: {vanCrClRatio} mg/day

interpretations.label: Normal renal function — q12h dosing appropriate
Mild-moderate renal impairment — extend to q24h
Severe renal impairment — q48h, monitor trough

calculators.source: Rybak et al. Therapeutic monitoring of vancomycin for serious methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infections: A revised consensus guideline. Am J Health-Syst Pharm. 2020;77(11):835-864.

calculators.name: Gentamicin Once-Daily (Extended Interval)
calculators.description: Hartford nomogram-style once-daily gentamicin dosing. 5-7 mg/kg q24h, interval extended for renal impairment. Target peak 16-24 mg/L.
calculators.transparency_template:
Gentamicin once-daily (Hartford nomogram):
Weight: {gentWeight} kg × {gentDosePerKg} mg/kg = {gentDose} mg (round to nearest 40mg = {gentDaily})
CrCl: {gentCrCl} mL/min → q{gentInterval}h

Target peak (1h post-dose): 16-24 mg/L
Target trough (pre-dose): <1 mg/L
Avoid >3 days if no culture clearance
Monitor for nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity
calculators.source: Nicolau DP et al. Experience with a once-daily aminoglycoside program administered to 2,184 adult patients. Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 1995;39(3):650-655.

calculators.name: Amikacin Dosing
calculators.description: Amikacin 15 mg/kg q24h for most infections, 25 mg/kg for severe. Adjust for renal impairment. Used in TB and resistant Gram-negative infections.
calculators.transparency_template:
Amikacin dosing:
{amiWeight} kg × {amiDosePerKg} mg/kg = {amiDose} mg IV q{amiInterval}h
CrCl: {amiCrCl} mL/min

Target peak: 40-50 mg/L (severe), 20-30 mg/L (moderate)
Trough: <5 mg/L (extended interval)
Monitor SCr daily. Ototoxicity risk.
calculators.source: Bartal K et al. Pharmacokinetic dosing of aminoglycosides: A controlled trial. Am J Med. 2003;114(3):194-198.

calculators.name: Enoxaparin Prophylaxis (VTE)
calculators.description: VTE prophylaxis dosing by CrCl and weight. Standard 40 mg SC daily; reduced for renal impairment; OBGYN-specific for caesarean.
calculators.transparency_template:
Enoxaparin prophylaxis:
CrCl: {enoxCrCl} mL/min
Weight: {enoxWeight} kg
Indication: {enoxIndication} mg

Dose: {enoxDose} mg SC q{enoxInterval}h

Caution:
- CrCl <20: avoid (accumulation risk)
- BMI ≥40: consider 40 mg q12h or 60 mg q24h
- Pregnancy: 40 mg q24h accepted

interpretations.label: Severe renal impairment — avoid or use UFH

calculators.source: NICE NG89. Venous thromboembolism in over 16s: reducing the risk. 2019 (updated 2024).

calculators.name: Enoxaparin Treatment Dose
calculators.description: Treatment-dose enoxaparin 1 mg/kg q12h or 1.5 mg/kg q24h, adjusted for CrCl. Used in DVT, PE, ACS.
calculators.transparency_template:
Enoxaparin treatment:
{enoxTxWeight} kg × {enoxTxRegimen == 12 ? "1" : "1.5"} mg/kg = dose
CrCl: {enoxTxCrCl} mL/min

Standard: 1 mg/kg q12h OR 1.5 mg/kg q24h
CrCl-adjusted: 50% dose if CrCl 20-30, avoid if <20
calculators.source: Garcia DA et al. Parenteral anticoagulants: Antithrombotic Therapy and Prevention of Thrombosis, 9th ed. ACCP. Chest. 2012;141(2 Suppl):e24S-e43S.

calculators.name: Warfarin Loading (Fennerty Nomogram)
calculators.description: Fennerty 10 mg loading nomogram for inpatient warfarin initiation. Outpatient 5 mg is preferred for most patients.
calculators.transparency_template:
Warfarin Fennerty nomogram:
Day {warfarinDay} | INR: {warfarinINR}

Day 1: 10 mg
Day 2: INR<1.5→10mg, 1.5-1.9→5mg, 2-2.4→2.5mg, ≥2.5→0
Day 3: INR<2→10mg, 2-2.4→5mg, 2.5-2.9→2.5mg, ≥3→0
Day 4-5: adjusted by INR

Target INR: 2-3 (most indications), 2.5-3.5 (mechanical valve)
Caution: avoid in pregnancy
calculators.source: Fennerty A et al. Flexible induction dose regimen for warfarin and prediction of maintenance dose. BMJ. 1984;288(6423):1268-1270.

calculators.name: Acetylcysteine (NAC) for Paracetamol Overdose
calculators.description: Prescott 21-hour IV NAC protocol for paracetamol overdose. Dosing by weight. Standard 3-bag regimen.
calculators.transparency_template:
Acetylcysteine (NAC) Prescott 21-hour regimen:

Bag 1: 150 mg/kg in 200 mL over 1 hour = {nacLoad} mg
Bag 2: 50 mg/kg in 500 mL over 4 hours = {nacBag2} mg
Bag 3: 100 mg/kg in 1000 mL over 16 hours = {nacBag3} mg

Total: 300 mg/kg over 21 hours = {nacTotal} mg

Indications: paracetamol level on Rumack-Matthew nomogram above treatment line, OR >150 mg/kg ingested with delayed presentation.

interpretations.label: Low weight — use smaller bags, monitor fluid overload
High weight — cap at 110 kg for dose calculation

calculators.source: Prescott LF et al. Intravenous N-acetylcysteine: the treatment of choice for paracetamol poisoning. BMJ. 1979;2(6198):1097-1100.

calculators.name: Subcutaneous Insulin Correction Dose
calculators.description: Sliding-scale insulin correction based on current BG and insulin sensitivity factor. Add to basal or TDD.
calculators.transparency_template:
Insulin sliding scale correction:

Correction = (Current BG − Target BG) ÷ Sensitivity
            = ({insBG} − {insTarget}) ÷ {insTDDRatio}
            = {insCorrection} units SC

Where 1800-rule: Sensitivity = 1800 ÷ TDD (rapid-acting insulin)
Or 1500-rule: 1500 ÷ TDD (regular insulin)

Apply before meals, hold if BG <70 mg/dL.
calculators.source: Umpierrez GE et al. Management of hyperglycemia in hospitalized patients in non-critical care setting: an Endocrine Society clinical practice guideline. J Clin Endocrinol Metab. 2012;97(1):16-38.

calculators.name: PESI / sPESI (PE Severity)
calculators.description: Pulmonary Embolism Severity Index. Stratifies 30-day mortality risk. PESI Class I-II = low risk (outpatient); sPESI = simplified version.

inputs.label: Heart failure or chronic lung disease

results.label: sPESI (simplified)
results.formula: pesiAge + pesiMale + pesiCancer + pesiCHF + pesiHR + pesiSBP

interpretations.label: Low risk (Class I-II) — outpatient candidate
interpretations.detail: PESI I-II. Mortality 0.6-3.5%. Consider outpatient management with LMWH/DOAC if haemodynamically stable, no other comorbidities, reliable follow-up.

interpretations.label: High risk (Class IV-V) — admit, consider ICU/thrombolysis
interpretations.detail: PESI IV-V. Mortality up to 24%. Haemodynamic monitoring, consider thrombolysis if shock.

calculators.source: Aujesky D et al. Derivation and validation of a prognostic model for pulmonary embolism. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2005;172(8):1041-1046.

calculators.name: SpO₂/FiO₂ Ratio (SF Ratio)
calculators.description: SF ratio as non-invasive surrogate for PaO₂/FiO₂ when ABG unavailable. Used in ARDS screening where ABG delayed.
calculators.transparency_template:
SF ratio = SpO₂/FiO₂ = {sfSpO2}/{sfFiO2} = {sfRatio}

Berlin definition ARDS (PaO₂/FiO₂):
  Mild: 200-300
  Moderate: 100-200
  Severe: <100

SF ratio equivalents (Rice et al 2007):
  SF≥315 ≈ P/F>300 (no ARDS)
  SF 235-314 ≈ P/F 200-300 (mild)
  SF 148-234 ≈ P/F 100-200 (moderate)
  SF <148 ≈ P/F <100 (severe)
calculators.source: Rice TW et al. Comparison of the SpO2/FIO2 ratio and the PaO2/FIO2 ratio in patients with acute lung injury or ARDS. Chest. 2007;132(2):410-417.

calculators.name: Partogram Action Line
calculators.description: Friedman curve action line calculation. Alert line at 1 cm/hr cervical dilation from 4 cm. Action line 4 hours to the right.

inputs.label: Time at 4 cm (hours since)
inputs.hint: How long ago she was at 4 cm dilation

calculators.transparency_template:
Partogram (Friedman):
At 4 cm: time 0
Expected progress: {partParity} cm/hr ({partParity == 1.2 ? "nullip" : "multip"})
Current: {partDilatation} cm after {partStartTime}h

Alert line: dilation at 1 cm/hr from 4 cm
Action line: 4h right of alert

If current cervical dilation is behind action line → protracted/arrested labour → consider oxytocin augmentation or operative delivery.

interpretations.label: On or ahead of alert line — continue observation
Between alert and action lines — monitor closely
Past action line — protracted/arrested labour
interpretations.detail: Past action line. Consider oxytocin augmentation if no progress for 2h. If >4h no progress with adequate contractions → operative delivery.

calculators.source: Friedman EA. Primigravid labor; a graphicostatistical analysis. Obstet Gynecol. 1955;6(6):567-589.

calculators.name: Paediatric ORS Replacement (WHO)
calculators.description: WHO Plan A/B/C for oral rehydration in children with diarrhoea. Plan A: no dehydration, Plan B: some, Plan C: severe.

results.label: Maintenance + losses
results.unit: mL/day (Plan A: 10 mL/kg per diarrhoeal stool)

calculators.transparency_template:
WHO ORS protocol:
Weight: {orsWeight} kg, Age: {orsAge} months
Plan: {orsPlan == 1 ? "A — no dehydration" : (orsPlan == 2 ? "B — some dehydration (75 mL/kg over 4h)" : "C — severe dehydration (IV)")}

Plan A: 10 mL/kg per loose stool + maintenance
Plan B: 75 mL/kg over 4 hours + 10 mL/kg/stool
Plan C: IV — 30 mL/kg in 1h (infant) or 30 min (older child), then 70 mL/kg over 5h

Deficit: {orsDeficit} mL
Hourly (Plan B): {orsHourly} mL/h
Maintenance (Plan A): {orsMain} mL/day

interpretations.label: Plan A — home ORS, return if worsens
Plan B — supervised ORS over 4h, reassess
Plan C — severe — IV fluids, hospitalise

calculators.source: WHO. The treatment of diarrhoea: a manual for physicians and other senior health workers. 2005.

calculators.name: Paediatric Fluid Bolus (Resuscitation)
calculators.description: 20 mL/kg crystalloid bolus for paediatric resuscitation. PALS / WHO ETAT guidelines. Repeat up to 3 boluses then reassess.
calculators.transparency_template:
Paediatric fluid bolus:
{pbWeight} kg × {pbBolusVol} mL/kg × {pbBolusCount} bolus(es)
= {pbBolus} mL per bolus
= {pbTotal} mL cumulative

Use NS or RL over 5-10 minutes.
Reassess after each bolus.
If still shocked after 3 boluses:
  - Consider inotropes
  - Consider blood (10-20 mL/kg if anaemic)
  - Consider colloids (controversial)

interpretations.label: 1st bolus — give now, reassess in 5 min
2nd bolus — reassess, look for source of shock
3rd bolus — likely refractory shock, ICU/transfer
interpretations.detail: After 3 boluses (60 mL/kg) — likely refractory. Start inotropes (dopamine 10 mcg/kg/min). Consider blood if anaemic. ICU-level care.

calculators.source: PALS 2020. Topjian AA et al. Part 4: Pediatric Basic and Advanced Life Support. Circulation. 2020;142(16_suppl_2):S469-S523.

calculators.name: SAPS II (Simplified Acute Physiology Score II)
calculators.description: ICU mortality prediction. 12 variables, score 0-163. Used for benchmarking and severity adjustment. Older than SAPS 3 but more familiar.

results.label: SAPS II score
results.formula: sapsAge + sapsHR + sapsSBP + sapsTemp + sapsGCS + sapsRR + sapsO2 + sapsUrine + sapsBUN + sapsHct + sapsK + sapsNa + sapsHCO3

interpretations.label: Very high mortality (>85%) — goals of care

calculators.source: Le Gall JR et al. A new Simplified Acute Physiology Score (SAPS II) based on a European/North American multicenter study. JAMA. 1993;270(24):2957-2963.

calculators.name: LACE Index (Readmission Risk)
calculators.description: LACE index predicts 30-day readmission/death. Useful for discharge planning. L=Length of stay, A=Acuity (admission type), C=Comorbidity (Charlson), E=Emergency visits.
calculators.transparency_template:
LACE Index (van Walraven 2010):
L (Length): {laceL} | A (Acuity): {laceA}
C (Charlson): {laceC} | E (ED visits): {laceE}

Total: {laceTotal}/19

Risk of 30-day readmission/death:
0-4: Low (~3%)
5-9: Moderate (~7%)
10-14: High (~15%)
≥15: Very high (~30%)

interpretations.label: Moderate — consider follow-up appointment within 7 days
Very high — case management, early clinic visit

calculators.source: van Walraven C et al. Derivation and validation of an index to predict early death or unplanned readmission after discharge from hospital to the community. CMAJ. 2010;182(6):551-557.

calculators.name: Hyponatraemia Correction Rate
calculators.description: Calculates safe correction rate for chronic hyponatraemia (max 8-10 mEq/L per 24h to avoid osmotic demyelination).
calculators.transparency_template:
Hyponatraemia correction safety:
Current Na⁺: {hypoNaCurrent}
Target: {hypoNaTarget} (Δ = {hypoDeltaNa} mEq/L)
Over: {hypoNaHours} hours

Max safe: {hypoMaxRate} mEq/L per hour
Max 24h: {hypoMax24} mEq/L (don't exceed 10)

Chronic hyponatraemia (>48h): correct slowly (≤8 mEq/L per 24h) to avoid osmotic demyelination syndrome (ODS).
Acute (<48h): may correct faster (1-2 mEq/L per hour).

interpretations.label: Rapid correction — risk of ODS
interpretations.detail: Risk of osmotic demyelination syndrome. Slow down. Recheck Na every 4-6 hours. Consider D5W ± DDAVP if over-correcting.

calculators.source: Verbalis JG et al. Diagnosis, evaluation, and treatment of hyponatremia: expert panel recommendations. Am J Med. 2013;126(10 Suppl 1):S1-S42.

calculators.name: Framingham Heart Failure Risk
calculators.description: 10-year risk of congestive heart failure in asymptomatic adults. Useful for primary prevention counselling.
calculators.transparency_template:
Framingham CHF Risk:
Age: {frmAge} (≥60 baseline 25%) | Male: {frmMale}
SBP≥160: +5 | BMI≥35: +4 | DM: +5 | Smoking: +2 | LVH: +4

Points: {frmRounded}

<5: Low (<10% in 10y)
5-9: Moderate (10-20%)
10-14: High (20-30%)
≥15: Very high (>30%)

Note: This is an approximation of the published multivariable model.
calculators.source: Kannel WB et al. Profile for estimating risk of heart failure. Arch Intern Med. 1999;159(11):1197-1204.

calculators.name: Y-Site IV Compatibility (Common)
calculators.description: Quick reference for common Y-site IV drug compatibility in ICU. Avoids physical precipitation and drug inactivation.

results.label: Y-site compatibility
results.formula: compatDrug1 == compatDrug2 ? 3 : ((compatDrug1 == 1 && (compatDrug2 == 2 || compatDrug2 == 6 || compatDrug2 == 7 || compatDrug2 == 11 || compatDrug2 == 12)) || (compatDrug2 == 1 && (compatDrug1 == 2 || compatDrug1 == 6 || compatDrug1 == 7 || compatDrug1 == 11 || compatDrug1 == 12)) || (compatDrug1 == 2 && compatDrug2 == 6) || (compatDrug2 == 2 && compatDrug1 == 6)) ? 1 : ((compatDrug1 == 14 && compatDrug2 == 18) || (compatDrug2 == 14 && compatDrug1 == 18)) ? 2 : 0)

calculators.transparency_template:
Y-site IV compatibility:
Drug A (id={compatDrug1}) + Drug B (id={compatDrug2})

COMPATIBLE: standard IV fluid, same line OK
INCOMPATIBLE: separate lines required (precipitation/inactivation)
CAUTION: can co-administer with monitoring
SAME: no need to combine

This is a simplified reference. Always check local pharmacy guidelines for full compatibility data.

interpretations.label: INCOMPATIBLE — separate lines or flush between
interpretations.detail: Risk of precipitation or drug inactivation. Use separate IV lumens or flush line with NS between drugs.

calculators.source: ASHP. Handbook on Injectable Drugs. 19th ed. 2017. Trissel.

calculators.name: Phenytoin Loading Dose (Status Epilepticus)
calculators.description: IV phenytoin loading 15-20 mg/kg for status epilepticus. Max infusion rate 50 mg/min in adults, 1-3 mg/kg/min in paeds. Monitor for hypotension, bradycardia.
calculators.transparency_template:
Phenytoin loading (status epilepticus):
Weight: {phenytoinWeight} kg x {phenytoinDosePerKg} mg/kg = {phenytoinTotalDose} mg

Dilute in NS to 1 mg/mL = {phenytoinVolNS} mL
Infuse over at least {phenytoinInfusionTime} min (max 50 mg/min adult)
Monitor BP, ECG, infusion site

Maintenance 12h later: 5-7 mg/kg/day IV/PO divided q6-8h

interpretations.label: Long infusion - consider fosphenytoin if available

calculators.source: Glauser T et al. Evidence-Based Guideline: Treatment of Convulsive Status Epilepticus in Children and Adults. Epilepsy Curr. 2016;16(1):48-61.

calculators.name: Hyperkalaemia Treatment (Emergency)
calculators.description: Emergency treatment doses for hyperkalaemia. Calcium gluconate for membrane stabilisation, insulin+glucose shift, salbutamol nebulisation, kayexalate for elimination.
calculators.transparency_template:
Hyperkalaemia emergency treatment:

1. STABILISE (if ECG changes):
   Ca gluconate 10% {hyperkCalcium} mL IV over 2-5 min

2. SHIFT K+ into cells:
   Insulin {hyperkInsulin}U IV in {hyperkD50} mL D50 (give D50 FIRST if BG <250)
   + Salbutamol {hyperkSalb} mg nebulised
   Onset 15-30 min, lasts 4-6h. Monitor BG q1h for 6h.

3. ELIMINATE K+:
   Kayexalate {hyperkKayexalate} g PO/PR (onset 1-2h)
   Or haemodialysis if refractory / ESRD

interpretations.label: Low weight - use paediatric insulin dose 0.1 U/kg
High weight - cap at 100 kg for dose calculations

calculators.source: European Resuscitation Council Guidelines 2021. Special circumstances: hyperkalaemia.
